title: forge-7xu hotfix 2: MainPane needs sized wrapper for virtualized timelines
description:
PROBLEM: GitHub tabs render blank because MainPane returns ActivityTimeline / PrTimeline / etc directly without a wrapping flex-sized element. The virtualizer needs a parent with definite height; gets zero.

FIX: Wrap MainPane content in a single <main className=ide-main> with flex:1 + min-height:0 + overflow:auto.

SCOPE: components/shell/MainPane.tsx, styles/globals.css (.ide-main rule).
notes:
Hotfix 2 done (Playwright-verified):
- MainPane.tsx wraps inner in <main className='ide-main' key=...> for sized parent + clean remount on swap.
- globals.css: html/body/#root height:100%; body:has(.ide-shell) overflow:hidden; .ide-shell height:100vh !important + overflow:hidden; .ide-main flex:1 + min-height:0 + overflow:hidden; .ide-main > * flex:1 + min-height:0.
- Verified: specialists/activity now shows full timeline with TODAY/YESTERDAY headers, ~100 events visible. shellHeight=823 matches viewport 823. mainHeight=783 (correctly constrained, scrollHeight=783).
close_reason: MainPane wrapped in sized .ide-main; shell height locked to 100vh. Specialists activity renders full timeline.

title: Fix production preview dashboard 404
description: PROBLEM: Running Gitboard preview on port 3010 returns 404 for /gitboard or /beadboard because dashboard routes are only enabled under NODE_ENV=production and built asset paths may not match the current Vite output location. SUCCESS: A single preview command serves /gitboard and /beadboard with built assets on port 3010. SCOPE: apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts and preview command/env only. NON_GOALS: No /console route, no UI redesign, no API behavior changes. CONSTRAINTS: Preserve existing API routes and production behavior; keep content types explicit. VALIDATION: build both dashboards, run server on 3010, verify /gitboard and /beadboard return 200 with non-empty HTML. OUTPUT: changed files, command to run, validation evidence.
close_reason: Done: preview server verified with NODE_ENV=production SKIP_GITHUB_POLLER=1 PORT=3010; /gitboard and /beadboard return 200.

title: Rollback xtrm console regressions
description: User reports xtrm console work broke existing Gitboard/Beadboard behavior and introduced regressions. Immediate remediation: restore application source to the last known-good state before console unification while preserving bead tracking history, rebuild dashboards, restart preview, and verify /gitboard and /beadboard function independently again.
close_reason: Done: rolled application source back to pre-console state, removed /console route/component files, rebuilt dashboards, and restarted preview with /gitboard and /beadboard functional.

title: forge-7xu hotfix: per-repo github fetch + feed shows all issues
description:
PROBLEM: Operator reports (1) Beads > Feed only shows open issues, should show all from Dolt (open + closed); (2) GitHub Activity/PRs/Issues all show 0 for most selected repos because the global API limit (50 events / 100 prs) returns only most-recent across ALL repos.

SUCCESS:
- Beads Feed tab includes closed issues alongside open
- GitHub tabs fetch per-repo data on (repo, tab) change instead of relying on globally-paginated store
- Tabs render real per-repo content for any selected repo

SCOPE: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/MainPane.tsx, components/beads/BeadsRepoView.tsx

NON_GOALS: No CSS changes. No backend changes.

CONSTRAINTS: Use existing apiClient.getEvents({repos:[..]}), getPrs({repo}), getIssues({repo}), getReleases({repo}). Don't regress beads tabs.

VALIDATION: Playwright — select gitboard repo on GitHub side, Activity tab shows events; Beads > Feed shows open + closed mixed.
notes:
Hotfix done (manual + Playwright):
- BeadsRepoView: switched to single listIssues({limit:1000}) call (backend /closed endpoint 404s on Dolt-backed projects). Split client-side by status. Verified Feed for /gitboard now shows 182 rows (172 closed + 9 open + 1 in-progress + 11 epics).
- GithubTabView: replaced global useGithubStore filtering with per-repo useGithubRepoData hook that fetches apiClient.getEvents({repos:[fullName],limit:200}) + getPrs({repo,limit:200}) + getIssues({repo,limit:200}) + getReleases({repo,limit:50}) on (repo, tab) change. Verified Activity for Jaggerxtrm/gitboard now shows 74 events.
close_reason: Per-repo github fetch + feed shows all Dolt issues. Playwright-verified 74 events / 182 beads rows.

title: forge-ci9 phase 2: topbar+tabs rebuild (full 7+5)
description:
Rebuild shell per operator spec after explorer inventory.

Layout:
- TopBar: [GitHub | Beads] switch + tabs for selected surface
- Sidebar: single-level repo list, RepoIcon octicons
- MainPane: renders (surface, tab, repo) view at FULL content

GitHub tabs (7): activity, prs, issues, releases, readme, changelog, reports
Beads tabs (5): kanban, feed, triage, closed, memories

Discard the file-tree Sidebar + per-leaf MainPane (forge-ci9.1). Drop Fira Code from chrome (sans-serif only). Keep black bg.

Port from beadboard App.tsx: ClosedIssuesPanel, MemoriesPanel, TriagePanel, filterIssues, matchesQuickFilter, isStaleIssue, buildTriageView, getVelocity, getHealthCounts helpers.
notes:
Rebuild done (manual + Playwright, 2026-05-16):

Layout: TopBar (surface switch + tab strip) + Sidebar (single-level, octicons, grouped) + MainPane (per-tab dispatch).

GitHub tabs (7): Activity / Pull Requests / Issues / Releases / README / CHANGELOG / Reports — all wired to existing components (ActivityTimeline/PrTimeline/IssueTimeline/ReleaseTimeline filtered by repo; ReadmeView/ChangelogView/ReportsView from RepoContentPanels via apiClient).

Beads tabs (5): Kanban / Feed / Triage / Closed / Memories — wired into BeadsRepoView which loads all data once and dispatches per-tab. Triage panel ported from beadboard App.tsx (buildTriage helper + TriagePanel with top picks/quick wins/stale/velocity/health). Closed + Memories ported as styled card lists. Feed uses existing IssueFeed.

Files written:
- types/shell.ts: Surface, GithubTab, BeadsTab unions + GITHUB_TABS/BEADS_TABS constants
- stores/shell.ts: setSurface/setTab/setRepo actions, selection persists
- hooks/useRepoTree.ts: now populates groupName for sidebar grouping
- components/shell/TopBar.tsx: surface switch + tab strip
- components/shell/Sidebar.tsx: single-level repo list, RepoIcon, groupName grouping, badge count
- components/shell/MainPane.tsx: dispatches per (surface, tab); EmptyState with recent-repo cards
- components/beads/BeadsRepoView.tsx: 5-tab dispatcher with TriagePanel/ClosedIssuesPanel/MemoriesPanel inline
- App.tsx: ShellApp uses TopBar + Sidebar + MainPane
- index.html: dropped Fira Code import (sans-serif only)
- globals.css: full .ide-* ruleset (TopBar, Sidebar, MainPane, triage, lists, skeleton) — black bg

Playwright-verified:
- Page renders with [GitHub|Beads] switch + 7/5 tabs
- Click repo → tab content loads (verified Issues swap)
- Surface switch [GitHub|Beads] → tab strip swaps, sidebar refilters (28 beads projects)
- Beads/Kanban shows real cards with priority + deps + status columns
- localStorage persists {surface, tab, repo}
close_reason: TopBar+tabs unified shell with full 7+5 tab parity. Playwright-verified GitHub/Beads switch + repo click + tab swap.

title: Explore: map every UI tab/view/feature in legacy beadboard frontend
description:
PROBLEM: forge-5w9 ported only kanban + overlay. Need full inventory of beadboard frontend.

SUCCESS: Markdown report listing every tab, view, panel, modal reachable from apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx and components/beads/*. For each: name, file:line, props/data needs, parent context, description.

SCOPE: apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx, components/beads/*, stores/beads.ts, lib/api.ts, styles/globals.css.

NON_GOALS: No edits. No gitboard. No design proposals.

CONSTRAINTS: READ_ONLY. Cite file:line.

VALIDATION: Output has table: Tab/View name | Component | File:line | Data source | Description.

OUTPUT: Markdown grouped by surface: 'Top-level layout', 'Project rail', 'Issue feed', 'Kanban', 'Issue overlay/dossier', 'Memories', 'Interactions', 'Other'. End with 'Features NOT ported into forge-5w9 BeadsRepoView' delta.
notes:
### Specialist Output — explorer (job 6f3c75) [WAITING — more output may follow]

Writing the full inventory report.

## Summary

Legacy beadboard has 6 primary tabs/surfaces with 18 components, 5 stores/data sources, and 3 modal/overlay surfaces. forge-5w9 ported only KanbanBoard + IssueOverlay — missing ProjectRail, IssueFeed (inline), TriagePanel, ClosedIssuesPanel, MemoriesPanel, and all header controls.

## Architecture Overview

**Top-level layout**: `App.tsx` controls tab routing, stores project selection state, orchestrates data loading. Three vertical regions: header (tabs + search + filters), ProjectRail sidebar, main content area.

**Component hierarchy**:
```
App.tsx
├── Header (tabs: issues/board/triage/closed/memories)
│   ├── ModuleSegments (5 tabs with counts)
│   ├── ModuleActions (search + filter toggle)
│   └── ModuleQuickFilters (all/ready/blocked/stale)
├── ProjectRail (sidebar)
│   └── ProjectRow (per-project stats + health)
└── Main content (tab-switched)
    ├── IssueFeed (tab: issues)
    │   └── IssueRow + IssueDossier (inline expansion)
    ├── KanbanBoard (tab: board)
    │   ├── StatusColumn (4 lanes)
    │   │   └── BeadCard
    │   └── IssueOverlay (portal)
    ├── TriagePanel (tab: triage)
    │   └── IssueRow + TriageStatCard
    ├── ClosedIssuesPanel (tab: closed)
    └── MemoriesPanel (tab: memories)
```

**Data flow**: Zustand store (`stores/beads.ts`) → API client (`lib/api.ts`) → REST endpoints (`/api/beads/projects/:id/*`).

## Risks

1. **Missing search/filter** — forge-5w9 users cannot filter board by status or search titles.
2. **No triage** — Users lose priority-based work recommendations.
3. **No project rail** — Multi-project workflows unsupported; must navigate repos individually.
4. **No PR linking** — No visibility into open PRs associated with issues.
5. **No audit trail** — No interaction history per issue.

description:
PROBLEM: forge-5w9 rebuild dropped features. Before rebuild, orchestrator needs complete inventory of what the LEGACY gitboard frontend rendered.

SUCCESS: Markdown report listing every tab, view, panel, modal, sub-component, and feature reachable from apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/* and App.tsx in its pre-forge-5w9 form (use 'git show origin/main:<path>' if needed). For each: name, file:line, props/data needs, parent context (which tab/route), description (1 line).

SCOPE: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx, components/github/* (GithubPanel, RepoSidebar, ActivityTimeline, PrTimeline, IssueTimeline, ReleaseTimeline, RepoContentPanels, RepoDossier, StatsHeader, EventDetail, EventIcon), hooks/, stores/github.ts, lib/client.ts. Inspect legacy form via 'git show origin/main:apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/<file>' for the App.tsx and GithubPanel.

NON_GOALS: No code edits. Do not catalogue beadboard. No design proposals.

CONSTRAINTS: READ_ONLY. Cite file:line for every claim.

VALIDATION: Output has table: Tab/View name | Component | File:line | Data source | Description.

OUTPUT: Markdown report grouped by top-level surface: 'Top-level layout', 'Sidebar', 'Activity tab', 'PRs tab', 'Issues tab', 'Releases tab', 'Repo dossier modal', 'Stats header', 'Other'. End with a 'Features dropped in forge-5w9' delta list.
notes:
### Specialist Output — explorer (job e5b0df) [WAITING — more output may follow]

Have all component files analyzed. Now compiling the legacy map.

## Summary

Legacy gitboard frontend (pre-forge-5w9) has **1 top-level tabbed shell** (`GithubPanel`) with **7 sub-tabs**, **1 resizable repo sidebar**, **4 timeline views**, **3 repo content views**, and **1 dossier modal**.

## Architecture overview

**Top-level surface**: `DashboardShell` in App.tsx wraps `GithubPanel` + `StatsHeader` + `RepoSidebar` in 3-column layout.

**Data flow**: Zustand store (`useGithubStore`) holds events/PRs/issues/releases/repos/summary. `useGithubActivity` hook fetches via `apiClient` on mount and subscribes to WebSocket for real-time updates.

**Virtualization**: All timeline views use `@tanstack/react-virtual` for performance.

**Key components**:
- `GithubPanel` — orchestrates sidebar + tabs + content
- `RepoSidebar` — filterable repo list with inline EventDetail
- `EventDetail` — collapsible commit accordion used in sidebar
- `ActivityTimeline` — virtualized event list with day headers + keyboard nav
- `PrTimeline` — PR rows with expand → conversation + files + linked beads
- `IssueTimeline` — issue rows with expand → body + labels
- `ReleaseTimeline` — release rows with expand → markdown body
- `RepoContentPanels` — README/CHANGELOG/Reports async render
- `RepoDossier` — modal overlay (portal to body) with repo dossier tabs

## Execution flows

App.tsx → DashboardShell → GithubPanel → { RepoSidebar + StatsHeader + TabBar + {ActivityTimeline|PrTimeline|IssueTimeline|ReleaseTimeline|ReadmeView|ChangelogView|ReportsView} }

## Key symbols

| `GithubPanel` | GithubPanel.tsx:99 | Main shell, manages tabs, data wiring |
| `TabBar` | GithubPanel.tsx:18 | 7-tab navigation bar |
| `RepoSidebar` | RepoSidebar.tsx:1 | Left rail repo list + resize + inline EventDetail |
| `ActivityTimeline` | ActivityTimeline.tsx:1 | Virtualized event feed |
| `PrTimeline` | PrTimeline.tsx:1 | PR list with detail hydration |
| `IssueTimeline` | IssueTimeline.tsx:1 | Issue list |
| `ReleaseTimeline` | ReleaseTimeline.tsx:1 | Release list |
| `RepoDossier` | RepoDossier.tsx:1 | Modal overlay |
| `useGithubStore` | stores/github.ts:1 | Zustand state |
| `useGithubActivity` | hooks/useGithubActivity.ts:1 | Data fetch + WS |

title: Epic: unify gitboard+beadboard into VS Code file-explorer shell
description:
PROBLEM: gitboard and beadboard are separate apps with separate sidebars, separate tabs, divergent styles. Operator wants to merge into one IDE-style shell where the left sidebar is a VS Code file-explorer-like tree of repos, each repo expandable to show /github and /beads children, click a child swaps the right pane.

VISION:
- Left sidebar = file-tree of repos. Each repo row: `/repo-name`            ●last-activity   <open-bead-count>
- Row uses tree branch glyph (|_ or ├─) for visual hierarchy under expanded repos.
- Click row → expand children indented:
    ├─ /github   ⊕PR  ⊕CO  ⊕IS  ⊕RE
    └─ /beads    ⊕OP  ⊕IP  ⊕BL  ⊕EP
  Both children have IDENTICAL chip count (4) for visual rhythm.
- Click /github → right pane shows that repo's gitboard (activity, PRs, issues, releases tabs collapse into one view).
- Click /beads → right pane shows that repo's beadboard (kanban + issue overlay).
- Sidebar must collapse/expand via a button (chevron at top of sidebar).
- Open in left collapsed state shows only icons. Selected child gets a left-edge accent rail (4px) + elevated bg.
- Aesthetic: minimal IDE — monochrome palette in tree, accent only for active row; keep current octicons; one font family.

INSPIRATION: https://events.iterate.com/streams/ — path-style row labels, indented children with + glyph, count badges, monochrome tree.

ARCHITECTURE: unify into apps/gitboard (option (a) from design discussion). Port apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/* into apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/. Add beads API client + Zustand store inside gitboard. Build new file-tree sidebar. Route-based right-pane swap. Deprecate apps/beadboard once parity verified.

SUCCESS:
- One Vite dev server (5173) serves the unified shell.
- Sidebar shows N repos as file-tree rows; expand/collapse works; chevron toggles overall sidebar collapse.
- Clicking /github or /beads swaps right pane; no full page reload.
- Beadboard kanban + issue overlay render inside gitboard's shell with identical token system.
- Recent activity octicons or chip row sits as second line below /repo name on the parent row (configurable; consider single-line variant too).
- apps/beadboard becomes a redirect or is deleted entirely.

SCOPE: apps/gitboard/* (new shell, new sidebar, ported beads components, routing); apps/beadboard/* (deprecate); packages/ui/src/styles/tokens.css (shared tokens — likely no new tokens needed); apps/gitboard/src/api/* (extend to proxy or aggregate beads data if needed).

NON_GOALS:
- No backend rewrite of beads data layer; beadboard's API on port 3010 stays; gitboard frontend talks to both.
- No new color palette; reuse existing tokens.
- No tab system at top — sidebar IS the navigation.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Preserve all current gitboard data fetching (github poller, releases, etc.) and beadboard data fetching.
- Preserve all current functionality on each child pane — kanban DnD, PR expansion, issue overlay, RepoDossier modal, etc.
- Same token system across both panes — radius, type scale, card pattern from forge-0zn epic.
- All octicons preserved; no emoji.

VALIDATION:
- Visual: full Playwright walkthrough — sidebar tree renders, expand/collapse, chevron toggle, right pane swap, kanban works, PR view works.
- Functional: no regressions in current data flow.
- Style: tree-row inspector shows monochrome palette except accent on active row; right pane consistent with sidebar tokens.

OUTPUT: unified app on 5173 with sidebar tree + dual right panes; apps/beadboard either redirect or deleted; updated CLAUDE.md mentioning unified shell.

CHILD ISSUE SHAPE (planner should split into):
- E1: design tokens audit + new sidebar tree tokens (if needed)
- E2: port beads components and types into gitboard
- E3: add beads API client + Zustand store in gitboard
- E4: build new file-tree Sidebar component (rows, expand/collapse, chevron, indentation glyphs, chip rows)
- E5: wire routing (repo + section → right pane swap)
- E6: integrate beadboard kanban + issue overlay into gitboard right pane
- E7: integrate gitboard activity/PRs/issues/releases into the /github right pane
- E8: collapse sidebar button + collapsed-state styling
- E9: deprecate apps/beadboard (redirect or delete)
- E10: README/CLAUDE.md updates
notes:
Epic complete (2026-05-16). All 10 children closed.

Implementation summary:
- forge-5w9.1: shell.ts types + routing decision (Zustand store-driven, no router lib)
- forge-5w9.2: beads-api client (same-origin /api/beads — gitboard already mounts beadboard routes)
- forge-5w9.3: useShellStore + useRepoTree aggregator (79 nodes verified: 69 github + 28 beads, 18 matched)
- forge-5w9.4: file-tree Sidebar with ARIA tree, keyboard nav, 22px compact rows, octicon chips zero-hidden
- forge-5w9.5: collapse toggle + mobile drawer pattern + localStorage persistence
- forge-5w9.6: Sidebar+MainPane swap at default /gitboard route; legacy shell at /gitboard/legacy
- forge-5w9.7: BeadsRepoView wrapping ported kanban (21 cards verified, card→overlay→Esc flow)
- forge-5w9.8: GithubRepoView with 4 stacked sections + sticky headers (Activity 11 / PRs 5 / Issues 0 / Releases 0 for /gitboard)
- forge-5w9.9: /beadboard → /gitboard redirect at server.ts (backend cross-import preserved)
- forge-5w9.10: CLAUDE.md IDE shell section

Review:
- Reviewer (forge-lec): PARTIAL 89/100. Only finding: getOpenPrs intentionally not ported — waiver memory saved.
- Security-auditor (forge-bvq): LOW finding (1) — encodeURIComponent missing on path segments. PATCHED same session.

Deferred follow-ups:
- Per-(repo,section) scroll/state preservation in MainPane (V1 uses key-remount).
- Hash router for deep links (#/r/owner/repo/github) — ~10 LOC if needed.
- Delete apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/* source entirely + drop dev:dashboard scripts.
- Move beadboard's api/core into gitboard to fully remove beadboard package.

description:
PROBLEM
1. Issues tab markdown not using renderPrBodyText; renders inconsistently vs PR/Release tabs.
2. Detail panes across gitboard tabs (PRs, Issues, Releases, README, CHANGELOG, Reports) have inconsistent widths. Beadboard uses clamp(560px, 75%, 1400px) centered — apply same pattern here.
3. README/CHANGELOG/Reports lack the PR-detail readable typography (lighter color, line-height, spacing).
4. Reports tab shows plain file list. Each report row should parse YAML frontmatter (--- session_date / branch / commits / issues_closed / specialist_dispatches ---) and show as compact meta in the collapsed row.

SCOPE: gitboard dashboard only.
close_reason: Consistent: gb-detail-stack class clamps PR/Issue/Release/README/CHANGELOG/Reports to 75% center. IssueExpandedBody now uses .pr-body-text + .pr-rich-text. Reports endpoint parses YAML frontmatter; rows show date/branch/commits/closed/filed/dispatch meta.

title: Repo content as tabs (not modal) + fenced code + table rendering
description: Drop RepoDossier modal. Move README/CHANGELOG/Reports into TabBar as sibling tabs to Activity/PRs/Issues/Releases. Extend renderPrBodyText to render fenced code blocks (```lang ... ```) including mermaid (as preformatted block) and markdown tables (| col | col |). Closes user feedback on forge-xnk.
close_reason: Tabs (README/CHANGELOG/Reports) added to gitboard TabBar; renderPrBodyText now handles fenced code + tables. RepoDossier kept for beadboard kanban reuse.

title: Releases tab in gitboard (integrate alongside Activity/PRs/Issues)
description:
PROBLEM
gitboard's TabBar in apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/GithubPanel.tsx has Activity/PRs/Issues but no Releases tab. GithubPoller already inserts ReleaseEvent rows into the events table. Need a Releases tab that visually integrates with the existing PrTimeline / IssueTimeline / ActivityTimeline style (same density, tokens, octicons, virtualizer pattern, dossier pattern).

REFERENCES (read these — do NOT recreate their primitives)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/GithubPanel.tsx (TabBar + Tab type + activeTab switch — extend)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/PrTimeline.tsx (canonical row + dossier shape; exports renderPrBodyText — IMPORT it, do NOT reimplement markdown rendering)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/IssueTimeline.tsx (sibling pattern for read-only timelines)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/ActivityTimeline.tsx (event-based timeline pattern)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/timeline-utils.ts (buildDateGroupedItems, formatRelativeTime, truncateBody, parseLabels — IMPORT, do NOT fork)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css (.pr-*, .issue-*, .activity-* row classes — REUSE these prefixes naming for .release-*; same tokens, same sizes, same density)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/github.ts (GithubState + useGithubStore — EXTEND this store, do NOT add a parallel one)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/client.ts (ApiClient class — ADD getReleases method following the getPrs/getIssues signature exactly)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/github.ts (existing /api/github/* endpoints — ADD /releases handler in this same file, do NOT create new router file)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/github-store.ts (getEvents/getRepos helpers — ADD getReleases(db, filters) here, do NOT create a new store module)
- apps/gitboard/src/types/github.ts (GithubEvent shape with type/repo/actor/title/body/url — ADD GithubRelease interface here, derive from ReleaseEvent payload)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts (verify ReleaseEvent payload shape — what fields are populated for type='ReleaseEvent' rows; tag/name/body live in payload)

SUCCESS
- New "Releases" tab appears in GithubPanel TabBar, sibling to Activity/PRs/Issues. Same styling (font, accent underline on active). Tab count shows current release count for the active filter.
- Clicking Releases renders <ReleaseTimeline releases={releases} /> in the same content area where PrTimeline/IssueTimeline mount.
- ReleaseTimeline.tsx:
  - Uses @tanstack/react-virtual virtualizer (mirror PrTimeline structure)
  - Uses buildDateGroupedItems + DayHeader from timeline-utils (mirror PrTimeline rendering of day groups)
  - Each row shows: TagIcon (left, --accent-blue/green tone), tag/name, repo, actor, relative time
  - Expand-in-place dossier: shows release body via renderPrBodyText (IMPORTED from PrTimeline), plus "Open on GitHub" external link
  - Empty state mirrors PrTimeline empty state (same dim text, same border)
- ReleaseTimeline test: smoke for empty + 1 row + expand renders body via the same renderPrBodyText path (mock via existing test patterns; see PrTimeline.test.tsx if it exists, else IssueTimeline.test.tsx)
- /api/github/releases endpoint: GET ?repo=...&limit=...&offset=... returns { releases: GithubRelease[] }. Default limit 50, ordered created_at DESC. 400 if no repo on bad params (mirror /api/github/prs error handling).
- github-store.ts: new getReleases(db, filters) selects events where type='ReleaseEvent', parses payload JSON to a GithubRelease shape.
- stores/github.ts: extends GithubState with `releases: GithubRelease[]`, `setReleases(...)`, fetched the same way activity/prs/issues are fetched (see existing fetch effects in App.tsx or whatever mounts the store).
- client.ts: new getReleases(params) following getPrs signature pattern.
- types/github.ts: new GithubRelease interface (id, tag_name, name, body, html_url, author_login, published_at, repo_full_name).

SCOPE
- NEW: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/ReleaseTimeline.tsx
- NEW: apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/components/github/ReleaseTimeline.test.tsx
- NEW: apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/github-releases.test.ts
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/GithubPanel.tsx (Tab type union + tabs[] entry + activeTab switch + count prop)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css (add .release-* rules MIRRORING .pr-* shape; reuse same tokens; no new palette)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/github.ts (add /releases handler in the existing createGithubRouter)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/core/github-store.ts (add getReleases fn)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/github.ts (add releases slice)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/client.ts (add getReleases method)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/types/github.ts (add GithubRelease interface)
- MODIFY: wherever the store fetches PRs/Issues on mount/repo-change — add a parallel fetchReleases call (orchestrator: check App.tsx or the panel for the existing fetch hooks; mirror their shape)

IMPERATIVE REUSE RULES (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
- IMPORT renderPrBodyText from PrTimeline.tsx; do NOT write a new markdown renderer.
- IMPORT buildDateGroupedItems, formatRelativeTime, truncateBody from timeline-utils.ts; do NOT inline copies.
- IMPORT @primer/octicons-react TagIcon; do NOT add other icon families.
- REUSE existing CSS tokens (var(--accent-*), var(--text-*), var(--border-*)); add NO new variables or palettes.
- EXTEND useGithubStore; do NOT create a parallel zustand store.
- ADD endpoint to existing createGithubRouter; do NOT create a new router file.
- ADD route handler to existing routes/github.ts; do NOT create routes/console.ts or any new router.
- New file count is bounded: exactly 3 new files (ReleaseTimeline.tsx, ReleaseTimeline.test.tsx, github-releases.test.ts). Everything else is an additive edit to an existing file.

NON_GOALS
- No /console app, no apps/console/ directory, no /api/console route group, no legacy redirects.
- No release CREATE/EDIT/DELETE; read-only.
- No assets list.
- No new shared package; everything inside apps/gitboard.
- No CSS in apps/beadboard.

CONSTRAINTS
- TypeScript strict mode (project default).
- Bun runtime; use Bun.serve / bun:sqlite where the surrounding code does.
- happy-dom env for React tests (matches existing dashboard tests).
- /api/github/releases must be added BEFORE any SPA catchall in server.ts (verify route order in the route group).
- Density: match .pr-row padding, row height, and font-size exactly.
- Mono: tag_name is mono (it's a sha/tag-like value), all other text uses var(--font-ui).
- Body: sanitised via renderPrBodyText (which already strips script/style/on*).

VALIDATION
- bun install at repo root.
- bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard test green; new ReleaseTimeline.test.tsx + github-releases.test.ts pass; existing 207 still pass (210 total expected).
- bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard build:dashboard green.
- bunx tsc --noEmit shows no new errors attributable to the touched files (preexisting repo-wide debt OK).
- Manual smoke (orchestrator runs after merge): GET /api/github/releases?repo=Jaggerxtrm/specialists returns ≥1 release; GET /api/github/releases (no repo) returns 400; gitboard dashboard shows Releases tab; clicking it lists rows; expanding renders body via renderPrBodyText.

OUTPUT
- File diff list (3 new, ~7 modified)
- /api/github/releases contract sample
- Confirmation that the tab visually matches PRs/Issues (density, tokens, octicon size)
- Any residual risks (release payload shape variance, virtualizer measureElement caveats)

title: Console: Releases module — fill the disabled chip with ReleaseTimeline
description:
PROBLEM
The /console scaffold has a disabled "Releases" chip in the right pane's module strip but clicking it does nothing. Release data is already polled into the events table by GithubPoller (type='ReleaseEvent'). forge-gg4's original spec mounted Releases inside legacy gitboard; instead, mount it inside /console (the new home).

REFERENCES
- Parent: forge-z6j (epic)
- Sibling shipped: forge-z6j.1 (scaffold), forge-z6j.2 (polish)
- Bead replacing in-place: forge-gg4 (Releases tab spec) — same intent, /console home instead of legacy gitboard
- apps/console/src/App.tsx (selectedRepo state, module chip strip — make Releases active)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/PrTimeline.tsx (exports renderPrBodyText — reuse for release notes markdown)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/IssueTimeline.tsx (timeline row + dossier pattern to mirror)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/github.ts (existing /api/github/* endpoints; add /releases here)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts (already inserts ReleaseEvent into events table; verify payload shape)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/github-store.ts (getEvents / getEventsByType helpers if present)
- apps/gitboard/src/types/github.ts (GithubEvent shape; add GithubRelease if needed)

SUCCESS
- New GET /api/github/releases?repo=owner/name returns rows from events table where type='ReleaseEvent', filtered by repo, ordered created_at DESC, limited 50 by default.
- New apps/console/src/modules/releases/ReleaseTimeline.tsx renders one row per release: tag + published date + author + click-to-expand inline dossier showing release notes via the markdown helper.
- Markdown helper: a small server-shared OR console-local SafeMarkdown that handles fenced code, bold/italic, links, headers, bullets, blockquotes, strips <script>/<style>/on*=. Either import the helper from gitboard (relative import like the merge route does) OR copy a minimal version into apps/console — pick whichever needs fewer cross-app imports while staying DRY.
- The "Releases" chip in /console's right pane becomes a button (no longer .is-disabled). Clicking it sets activeModule=releases and renders ReleaseTimeline below the modules strip.
- selectedRepo + activeModule both reflect in URL: ?repo=owner/name&module=releases. Refresh restores state.
- Other chips (Activity/PRs/Issues/Beads/Reports) stay disabled with a small "Coming" affordance to make scope clear — do not start implementing them.
- /api/github/releases returns 200 + [] when there are no releases for the repo; never throw.
- Existing apps/gitboard tests still green; new console test for the module switch + new gitboard test for the endpoint.

SCOPE
- NEW: apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/github.ts (add GET /releases handler — extend the existing router; do not fork the file)
- NEW: apps/console/src/modules/releases/ReleaseTimeline.tsx
- NEW: apps/console/src/modules/releases/release-types.ts (ConsoleRelease interface — superset of the payload subset we need)
- NEW: apps/console/src/lib/safe-markdown.ts (small renderer; mirror gitboard's renderPrBodyText behavior, no charts/code-highlighting beyond fences)
- MODIFY: apps/console/src/App.tsx (activeModule state, URL sync, chip→button for Releases, mount ReleaseTimeline)
- MODIFY: apps/console/src/api/client.ts (add getReleases(repo))
- MODIFY: apps/console/src/styles/globals.css (release row + dossier styles; reuse existing tokens; no new palette)
- NEW: apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/github-releases.test.ts (endpoint smoke with mocked db)
- NEW: apps/console/tests/modules/releases/ReleaseTimeline.test.tsx (renders empty state + renders a row + dossier expand)
- VERIFY ONLY: apps/console/package.json (likely needs vitest + happy-dom — add devDeps if not present; mirror beadboard's config)

NON_GOALS
- No release CREATE/EDIT actions; read-only.
- No semver grouping or chronology beyond DESC order.
- No assets list, no artifact downloads.
- No PRs/Issues/Activity/Beads/Reports modules — only Releases. Future beads handle those.
- No new icon family; @primer/octicons-react (TagIcon).
- No new color tokens.

CONSTRAINTS
- TypeScript strict mode.
- Octicons TagIcon for the release row glyph.
- Mono restricted to ids/sha/tag/branch — never on release name body.
- Single --accent token reuse.
- Body sanitisation must strip <script>, <style>, on*= attributes; mirror gitboard's renderPrBodyText.
- Endpoint must accept ?repo=owner/name (URL-encoded); 400 if missing repo; 200 + [] if repo exists but no events.
- Bun runtime: server-side code uses Bun.serve + bun:sqlite (consistent with apps/gitboard); helper code in apps/console runs in the browser.
- Tests in apps/console use happy-dom env (mirror beadboard). If vitest config missing, add minimal apps/console/vitest.config.ts copying beadboard's pattern.

VALIDATION
- `bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard test` green; new github-releases.test.ts passes.
- `cd apps/console && bun run build` green.
- `cd apps/console && bun run test` green (or wire scripts if missing).
- Manual smoke (orchestrator): /api/console/repos still works, /api/github/releases?repo=Jaggerxtrm/gitboard returns 200, /console with ?module=releases shows the empty-state when state.db has 0 release events, OR shows rows when it has some.

OUTPUT
- File diff list
- /api/github/releases contract (request/response shape)
- Confirmation that Releases chip becomes a button and the timeline renders
- Any residual risks (e.g. ReleaseEvent payload shape variance across GH event types)

title: Console rail polish: derive owner/repo for beads + display name + click-to-select
description:
PROBLEM
Post-z6j.1 the /console rail renders beads-only entries as UUIDs (e.g. "0494245c-...") and the github/beads merge never collides because beads ProjectScanner uses uuid as project.id while github uses owner/repo. Rows are also non-clickable so the empty right pane never changes. Result: the scaffold renders but feels broken to the user.

REFERENCES
- Parent: forge-z6j (epic)
- Sibling: forge-z6j.1 (scaffold; SHIPPED)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/console.ts (mergeConsoleRepos uses project.id as full_name — line 20 — wrong key)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts (ProjectScanner; check if it already reads git remote)
- apps/console/src/App.tsx (rail renders repo.full_name verbatim; no click handler)
- apps/console/src/types/console.ts (ConsoleRepoEntry shape)
- apps/console/src/styles/globals.css (existing .console-repo-row class)
- apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/console.test.ts (merge test to extend)

SUCCESS
- Beads project entries in /api/console/repos return a `full_name` derived from the project's git remote when available (`owner/repo` parsed from `git -C <path> config --get remote.origin.url` OR by reading `.git/config` directly — no new dependency). Fall back to `project.name` when no remote.
- When github and beads point at the same `owner/repo`, the response has ONE entry with both `github` and `beads` populated.
- New `display_name` field on ConsoleRepoEntry: github.display_name || beads.name || full_name (computed server-side).
- /console rail renders `entry.display_name` (NOT raw `full_name`), with `full_name` as a smaller mono subtitle when they differ.
- Clicking a rail row sets a `selectedRepo` state in App.tsx; the right pane shows the selected repo header (display_name + full_name + a "Modules" placeholder block listing future tabs as disabled chips: Activity, PRs, Issues, Releases, Beads, Reports).
- Existing tests still pass; new test asserts a beads project whose path has remote `Jaggerxtrm/gitboard` merges with the github repo of the same full_name.

SCOPE
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/console.ts (derive owner/repo from beads project; populate display_name; rewrite mergeConsoleRepos to key on derived full_name)
- MODIFY: apps/console/src/types/console.ts (add display_name?: string)
- MODIFY: apps/console/src/App.tsx (display_name in rail, click handler, selectedRepo state, right-pane header + disabled module chips)
- MODIFY: apps/console/src/styles/globals.css (active row state, subtitle style, repo header, disabled chip cluster)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/console.test.ts (cover remote-derivation merge + display_name)
- NEW (if needed): apps/gitboard/src/core/git-remote.ts (tiny helper: read .git/config or invoke `git -C <path> remote get-url origin`, return parsed owner/repo or null)

NON_GOALS
- No actual module content (PRs/Issues/Releases/Beads/Reports tabs still come from later beads — render disabled chips only).
- No edits to ProjectScanner core behavior (read-only helper; do not change project.id semantics in DB).
- No new deps. Use Bun.file / fs / child_process built-ins.
- No CSS framework swap.
- No new icon usage beyond what apps/console already imports.

CONSTRAINTS
- TypeScript strict mode.
- Octicons only (already imported); do not add other icon families.
- Mono font only on the small full_name subtitle when full_name has a "/" (i.e. looks like owner/repo); never on the display_name.
- Single --accent token.
- Git remote parsing must handle both `https://github.com/owner/repo.git` and `git@github.com:owner/repo.git`; strip trailing `.git`.
- If reading .git/config: use Bun.file (this is a Bun project, see apps/gitboard/CLAUDE.md). Avoid `child_process` if a file read suffices — faster + no shell escape risk.
- Selected repo state may be in a URL query param too (?repo=owner/repo) so deep links work; if scope permits, sync state with location.search.
- Disabled chips must use existing tokens (e.g. border-color: var(--border-muted); color: var(--text-muted)).

VALIDATION
- `bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard test` green; new merge test passes asserting a beads project at a path with remote Jaggerxtrm/gitboard merges with the github repo of full_name Jaggerxtrm/gitboard into one ConsoleRepoEntry with both `github` and `beads` set.
- `cd apps/console && bun run build` green.
- `bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard build` green.
- `bunx tsc --noEmit` shows no new errors attributable to scope files.
- Manual smoke (orchestrator runs): /api/console/repos response has at least 1 entry with BOTH github AND beads (will hit because Jaggerxtrm/gitboard exists both ways).

OUTPUT
- File diff list + key snippets
- /api/console/repos sample showing one merged entry (github+beads)
- Confirmation that the rail click sets the right pane and the disabled chips render
- Any residual risks (e.g. repos without remote stay keyed by project.name; non-github remotes show full URL or fall back)

title: Unify gitboard + beadboard under one /console shell
description:
PROBLEM
gitboard and beadboard run as two separate apps under apps/{gitboard,beadboard}/. The user has to switch URL/tab to move between GitHub work and beads work, and any shared chrome (rail, topbar, search, density) drifts between the two. A pre-existing bd memory describes a planned /console shell but no /console app exists in apps/.

REFERENCES
- bd memory: xtrm-console-now-uses-console-as-the-primary (stale until this epic lands)
- bd memory: xtrm-console-visual-contract-bg-181818-single-accent
- Current row + dossier primitives shipped on this branch: forge-l85, forge-wky, forge-zg3, forge-7n6, forge-8it
- Existing apps: apps/gitboard/ (Bun+Hono+Vite, port 3010), apps/beadboard/ (Vite-only, currently served via gitboard server in production mode)
- Existing mounting: apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts serves /gitboard, /beadboard SPA HTMLs when NODE_ENV=production

SUCCESS
- A new apps/console/ workspace builds and is mounted at GET /console by apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts.
- /console has one shell: single topbar (brand + repo search + status), single unified rail (GitHub repos + beads projects, merged by repo full_name), and module tabs: Activity · PRs · Issues · Releases · Beads (Feed/Board/Triage/Closed) · Reports.
- All modules render through the row + dossier primitives already on this branch (no per-module CSS drift).
- /gitboard and /beadboard remain mounted as legacy redirects to the corresponding /console module + repo filter.

SCOPE (this is the EPIC — child beads carry SCOPE files)
- apps/console/ (new workspace, vite+react)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts (add /console mount, redirect /gitboard /beadboard)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/console.ts (new: /api/console/repos merging /api/github/repos + /api/beads/projects keyed by repo full_name)
- packages/ui/src/styles/tokens.css (already canonical; no changes expected)

NON_GOALS
- No new auth.
- No backend re-architecture; keep Bun+Hono+single process model.
- No DB schema changes; merging happens at API layer.
- No removal of apps/gitboard or apps/beadboard until /console reaches parity (legacy redirects stay).
- No mobile-first rework; desktop dark UI only.

CONSTRAINTS
- Reuse existing components (IssueFeed, TriagePanel, PrTimeline, IssueTimeline, ActivityTimeline, RepoSidebar) — adapt props, don't fork.
- Single accent (--accent), no extra palettes.
- No charts (per existing contract).
- Octicons only (per forge-hl1).
- Mono restricted (per forge-m38).

VALIDATION

OUTPUT

title: Beadboard: dossier width inconsistent because module-list grid doesn't span
description: .module-list uses display:grid without grid-template-columns, so each implicit grid row sizes to fit content (longest child). Dossier 75% width is then computed against the row's narrower box, not viewport. Fix: grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1fr) (or switch to flex column).
close_reason: module-list now grid-template-columns minmax(0,1fr); rows fill row width

title: Beadboard refinements: single-line rows, text types, centered dossier, fix \n escape
description: User feedback bundle. (1) Row anatomy single-line by default — title and meta inline, wrap only on narrow viewports. (2) Replace type-mark Octicon with the actual text label (Bug/Task/Epic/Feature/Chore). (3) Expanded dossier: drop the right sidebar; single centered column ~720px max; order = Metadata → Description → Notes → Labels → Dependency tree. (4) Markdown: unescape literal '\\n' in description (some sources store JSON-escaped newlines). (5) Make sure backtick code and **bold** render in description, not only notes.
close_reason: Single-line rows, text type label, centered single-col dossier (metadata→description→labels→tree), JSON-escaped newline unescape

title: Beadboard: dep tree id wraps + child titles empty
description: Two issues visible after forge-7n6: (1) Long bead ids in the dep tree wrap to two lines and the title overlaps — need white-space:nowrap + overflow:hidden + ellipsis on .bead-dep-tree-id (or max-width). (2) Child dep rows show id + [kind] but title column is empty. Check API response for dependencies — may need to fetch full issue per dep id or accept that title can be empty and hide the title cell when blank.
close_reason: id no longer wraps; dep titles resolved from loaded issues map

title: Beadboard: show dep IDs + tree like bd dep tree
description: User feedback: rows show '1 deps' but not which bead. Mimic bd dep tree output. (1) In the row meta-right, replace 'N deps' with up to 2 short bead-id chips + kind hint (↪ for blocks, ↩ for blocked_by, ⊃ for parent-child). (2) In the dossier, add a 'Dependency tree' block: root = current issue, children = each dep with kind label like [blocks] [blocked_by] [parent-child] [discovered-from], status check/dot, tree connectors. (3) If a dep id matches a loaded issue, recurse one level. Octicons only; no charts.

title: Epic: Normalize Gitboard and Beadboard visual system
description:
## Overview

Normalize Gitboard and Beadboard to the xtrm Console Minimal FUI contract before any further shell unification. This is a visual-system convergence pass: both apps should look like tabs of one professional operations console while remaining separate, stable routes during the migration.

## Goals

- Gitboard and Beadboard share the same #181818 base, square edges, border language, typography, spacing, and row rhythm.
- PR rows and bead issue rows use the same list-first anatomy.
- PR expanded detail and bead expanded dossier use the same section rhythm.
- Existing /gitboard and /beadboard functionality remains stable.

## Non-goals

- No /console route resurrection in this epic.
- No iframe embedding.
- No shared repo rail yet.
- No backend data-model rewrite.
- No mutating GitHub or Beads actions.

## Success criteria

- [ ] docs/xtrm-console-visual-contract.md is followed.
- [ ] Gitboard and Beadboard no longer have conflicting palettes or rounded-card styling.
- [ ] Beadboard feed visually matches Gitboard PR/activity list spacing and shapes.
- [ ] Both dashboard builds pass.
- [ ] Preview confirms /gitboard and /beadboard remain functional independently.

## Context

Visual contract: docs/xtrm-console-visual-contract.md
Key areas: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard, apps/beadboard/src/dashboard

title: Create beadboard app - Beads Issue Tracking Dashboard
description:
Phase 2 of monorepo expansion:

## Goals
- Create apps/beadboard/ as a second dashboard for beads issue tracking
- Implement multi-project discovery (find .beads/ directories)
- Build Kanban board UI matching gitboard design system
- Add closed issues section
- Add memory panel (bd memories)
- Implement PR auto-linking from worktrees/branches
- Track agent sessions from interactions.jsonl

## Tech Stack (matching gitboard)
- Runtime: Bun
- HTTP: Hono
- UI: React 19 + Vite + Tailwind v4
- State: Zustand
- Icons: @primer/octicons-react
- Use @omniforge/core, @omniforge/ui packages

## Key Features
1. Project sidebar - list discovered beads projects
2. Kanban board - Open → In Progress → Blocked → Closed
3. Closed issues section - collapsible, shows recent closed
4. Memory panel - bd memories integration
5. Agent badges - show which agent worked on issue
6. PR links - auto-detect from worktrees and commits

## DDD Approach
- Domain: BeadIssue, BeadsProject, Memory, AgentSession, LinkedPr
- Application: ProjectScanner, BeadsReader, PrLinker, AgentTracker
- Infrastructure: SQLite metadata, dolt connection, file watcher
close_reason:
Beadboard app complete with all key features:
- Project sidebar: Discovers .beads/ directories via ProjectScanner
- Kanban board: 4-column layout (Open/In Progress/Blocked/Closed)
- Closed issues section: ClosedIssuesPanel with agent badges
- Memory panel: Reads from knowledge.jsonl
- Agent badges: Shows Claude/Qwen/Gemini badges on cards
- Real dolt connection: DoltClient queries live database
- 40 tests passing (core + store + dolt-client)

Remaining features filed as separate issues:
- forge-1kv: PR auto-linking from worktrees
- forge-yhm: WebSocket live events

title: Dolt connection stability — pool + health probe + reconnect + circuit breaker
description:
PROBLEM
forge-igg's BeadsChangeWatcher polls Dolt every 2s per active project via dolt-client. The current client opens a short-lived connection per query — fine for sporadic reads, fragile under continuous polling. Need a stable pooled client with health probes, reconnect, and a circuit breaker that flips watcher to JSONL fallback when Dolt is down.

REFERENCES
- apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts (existing client; add pool + probe + reconnect)
- apps/beadboard/src/core/beads-change-watcher.ts (forge-igg; consumer of the pool, surfaces source_health)
- apps/gitboard/src/types/realtime.ts (forge-dgd; beads:source_health payload shape)

SUCCESS
- Single shared connection pool (Bun-native MySQL-protocol driver) with max=N, idleTimeout aligned to Dolt server defaults. Replaces per-call connect/close.
- Health probe SELECT 1 every 5s on idle connections; evict on failure.
- Reconnect with exponential backoff 250ms→30s + jitter; emit beads:source_health { source:'dolt', healthy:false } while down, flip back on recovery.
- Circuit breaker: after N consecutive failures, pause Dolt polling for project, watcher flips to JSONL automatically (source:'jsonl').
- Per-query timeout (3s wrapper); surface timeouts as health degradation, not exceptions.
- If we launched dolt sql-server, restart on crash with backoff; if external, observe only.
- Metrics: in-memory ring of last 100 query latencies + failure count; expose at /api/internal/dolt-health.

SCOPE
- MODIFY apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts (pool, probe, reconnect, breaker, timeouts, metrics)
- MODIFY apps/beadboard/src/core/beads-change-watcher.ts (consume breaker state; flip source)
- NEW apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/internal-dolt-health.ts (read metrics; gated to localhost)
- NEW apps/beadboard/tests/core/dolt-client-pool.test.ts (probe, reconnect, breaker open/half-open/closed)
- NEW apps/beadboard/tests/core/dolt-kill-smoke.test.ts (start watcher, kill dolt sql-server, verify source flip + reconnect + flap)

NON_GOALS
- No schema migration.
- No multi-Dolt-host failover.
- No persistent metrics store (in-memory only).
- No new Dolt driver dependency unless current one cannot pool — prefer extending existing.

CONSTRAINTS
- TypeScript strict, Bun runtime.
- Pool size + timeouts as constants at top of dolt-client.ts.
- All existing dolt-client callers must keep working unchanged (signature-compatible).
- Health endpoint must NOT leak credentials or query bodies.

VALIDATION
- bun run lint + tests green.
- Smoke: start server, observe steady-state pool usage; kill dolt sql-server; verify source_health flips and JSONL takes over within 5s; restart dolt; verify recovery and source_health flips back.

OUTPUT
- File diffs
- Pool config table (size, timeouts, breaker thresholds) with rationale
- Failure mode matrix (Dolt down / slow / partial / restart)
notes: Scope confirmation (pre-claim): dolt-client.ts already uses mysql2/promise. Pool migration is createConnection → createPool, NOT a driver swap. Existing public API (connect/disconnect/isConnected/getIssues/...) stays signature-compatible; pool replaces the single connection field. mysql2 pool exposes built-in idle timeout + keep-alive; health probe wraps pool.query('SELECT 1') on schedule.

title: Realtime protocol contract module (types + channels + seq/ring buffer + resume)
description:
PROBLEM
forge-f0g (live GitHub) and forge-igg (live Beads) both push events over the existing WS ChannelRegistry but there is no shared envelope, no event-name catalogue, no sequence numbers, no replay path, and no protocol version. Implementing each in isolation will produce divergent payloads and brittle clients. Lock the contract first so downstream beads consume it.

REFERENCES
- apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/channels.ts (ChannelRegistry; widen ChannelName, add seq + ring buffer)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/handler.ts (WsHandler; add 'resume' action)
- apps/gitboard/src/types/{github.ts,beads.ts} (payload shapes referenced from realtime types)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/ws.ts (client; resume support)

SUCCESS
- NEW apps/gitboard/src/types/realtime.ts exporting:
  - REALTIME_PROTOCOL_VERSION = 1
  - RealtimeEnvelope<E,D> { type:'event', channel, event, seq, ts, version, data }
  - GithubRealtimeEvent union: 'github:pr.upsert'|'github:issue.upsert'|'github:event.append'|'github:release.upsert'|'github:sync_hint'|'github:rate_limit'
  - BeadsRealtimeEvent union: 'beads:issue.upsert'|'beads:issue.close'|'beads:issue.delete'|'beads:issue.deferred'|'beads:issue.superseded'|'beads:issue.flagged'|'beads:issue.unflagged'|'beads:dep.upsert'|'beads:dep.delete'|'beads:memory.upsert'|'beads:memory.delete'|'beads:kv.upsert'|'beads:kv.delete'|'beads:comment.append'|'beads:batch'|'beads:sync_hint'|'beads:source_health'
  - Per-event payload type aliases (GithubPrUpsert, BeadsIssueUpsert, etc.)
- channels.ts widens ChannelName with template literals: `github:repo:${string}`, `beads:project:${string}`. publish() adds monotonic seq + ts + version; maintains per-channel ring buffer (last 500 envelopes).
- handler.ts handles 'resume' action: { action:'resume', channel, since_seq } → server replays buffered envelopes >since_seq, or emits {event:'<domain>:sync_hint', reason:'buffer_miss'} when out of range.
- Server rejects subscribe from clients with mismatched protocol major (close code 4001).
- Client lib/ws.ts: track last seq per channel in-memory; auto-send resume on reconnect.
- Backward compatibility: legacy 'new_event' / 'new_commits' event names aliased server-side for one release.

SCOPE
- NEW apps/gitboard/src/types/realtime.ts
- MODIFY apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/channels.ts (ChannelName widen, publish seq+buffer, replay helper)
- MODIFY apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/handler.ts (resume action, version handshake)
- MODIFY apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/ws.ts (seq tracking, resume on reconnect)
- NEW apps/gitboard/tests/api/ws/realtime-contract.test.ts (envelope shape, seq monotonic, ring buffer replay, version mismatch close)

NON_GOALS
- No GitHub or Beads event producers (forge-f0g, forge-igg).
- No persistence of ring buffer across restarts.
- No multi-process fanout.

CONSTRAINTS
- TypeScript strict.
- Bun runtime; no new deps.
- Ring buffer size constant at top of channels.ts.
- Envelope must be JSON-serialisable.

VALIDATION
- bun run lint + bun run test green.
- Manual: connect ws, subscribe, drop connection, reconnect with resume — verify backfill or sync_hint.

OUTPUT
- File diffs
- Final TS type definitions (the API contract)
- Notes on backward compat window

  - `git diff --name-only ...`
  - pattern search for `dangerouslySetInnerHTML|innerHTML|localStorage|WebSocket|VITE_BEADS_API_URL|projectId|issueId`
  - file reads + line-number inspection with `nl -ba`

## Status

findings

## Findings

- ID: F-01
- Severity: low
- Category/CWE when applicable: CWE-20 / CWE-116
- Evidence:
  - `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/beads-api.ts:55`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/beads-api.ts:63`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/beads-api.ts:74`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/beads-api.ts:81`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/beads-api.ts:89`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/beads-api.ts:96`
- Reachability/exploitability:
  - `projectId` comes from remote `listProjects()` result, then reused in later path construction through `useRepoTree` and `BeadsRepoView`.
  - `issueId` comes from remote issue data, then reused in `getIssue()`.
  - Path segments interpolate raw values, not `encodeURIComponent(...)`.
  - Crafted IDs containing `/`, `?`, `#`, `%2f`-style edge cases can change request path semantics client-side.
- Impact:
  - Wrong same-origin endpoint fetch.
  - Possible route confusion / broken object lookup.
  - No direct XSS shown from this flaw alone.
- Recommended fix:
  - Encode path segments before interpolation. Example: `encodeURIComponent(projectId)` and `encodeURIComponent(issueId)` for every path segment.
  - Keep query params in `URLSearchParams`, already done for `issue_id` search path.
- Verification after fix:
  - Confirm requests for IDs containing reserved chars hit intended endpoint only.
  - Add unit test for IDs with `/`, `?`, `#`, `%2F`.

## Dependency Advisory Triage

No dependency audit run. Task scoped to source diff only.

## Non-findings / Dropped Noise

- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/IssueFeed.tsx:204-340` safe markdown path. No `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`. Output built as React nodes, not raw HTML.
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/Sidebar.tsx:219-231`, `MainPane.tsx`, `GithubRepoView.tsx` render store/API strings through normal JSX text nodes. No direct DOM HTML sink.
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/shell.ts:15-34` localStorage persistence exists, but values only drive selection/collapse state. No code execution sink found.
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/beads-api.ts:28-31` `VITE_BEADS_API_URL` use not finding by itself. Build-time/operator-controlled config, not user-controlled in this scope.
- Websocket handling not present in scoped files. Search hit only out-of-scope `lib/ws.ts` and `hooks/useWebSocket.ts`.

## Changes

None. Read-only audit.

- Searched scoped dashboard files for HTML injection sinks, storage, env use, websocket references.
- Read all scoped target files.

- Residual trust in upstream API data remains, but no direct XSS sink found in scoped diff.

## Summary

gitboard: GitHub activity dashboard (events, PRs, issues, releases) with sidebar repo selection. beadboard: Beads issue tracker (feed, kanban, triage) with project-based data model. Both apps share `packages/ui` tokens but diverge in tsconfig (`allowImportingTsExtensions` vs `paths`). Key collision risk: `types/github.ts` vs `types/beads.ts`, separate stores (`stores/github.ts` vs `stores/beads.ts`), distinct API layers (`api/routes/github.ts` vs `api/routes/beads.ts`). Port order: tokens (shared) → gitboard App entry → beadboard components → aggregator hook.

## 4. Port Risks

### Risk 1: tsconfig Divergence

| Setting | gitboard | beadboard | Impact |
|---------|----------|-----------|--------|
| `allowImportingTsExtensions` | `true` | **absent** | gitboard can import `.tsx` directly in types; beadboard cannot |
| `paths` | absent | `{ "@/*": ["./src/*"] }` | beadboard uses `@/` aliases; gitboard uses relative imports |

**Mitigation:** Align on one config. Recommend `allowImportingTsExtensions: true` (simpler) + drop `paths` (use relative imports consistently).

### Risk 2: Cross-Imports That Will Break

| Import Path | Source | Breaks When |
|-------------|--------|-------------|
| `import type { SidebarSelection } from "../types/shell.ts"` | gitboard `App.tsx:5` | **Immediate** — file doesn't exist yet (forge-5w9.1 artifact) |
| `import { useBeadsStore } from "./stores/beads.ts"` | beadboard `App.tsx` | If merged into gitboard, path changes to `../stores/beads.ts` |
| `import type { BeadIssue } from "../types/beads.ts"` | beadboard components | Path changes if merged into gitboard tree |
| `import { api } from "./lib/api.ts"` | beadboard components | Path changes if merged |

**Pattern:** All beadboard imports use relative paths `./` and `../`. After merge, `./` becomes `./beads/` or similar, breaking all local imports.

### Risk 3: Shared Symbol Collisions

| Symbol | gitboard | beadboard | Collision Risk |
|--------|----------|-----------|----------------|
| `Status` type | — | `types/beads.ts:5` | None — gitboard doesn't have this |
| `Priority` type | — | `types/beads.ts:6` | None — gitboard doesn't have this |
| `App` component | `dashboard/App.tsx` | `dashboard/App.tsx` | **COLLISION** — both export `App` |
| `useStore` pattern | `useGithubStore` | `useBeadsStore` | No collision — different names |
| `globals.css` | 2803 lines | 206 lines | Both import `tokens.css`; beadboard adds `.module-*` classes |

### Risk 4: State Store Isolation

- gitboard: Zustand store in `stores/github.ts` with 14 slice actions
- beadboard: Zustand store in `stores/beads.ts` with 11 slice actions
- **Both are independent** — no collision, but need namespacing in unified shell

### Risk 5: API Layer Isolation

- gitboard: `api/routes/github.ts` — 350+ lines, GitHub REST API + SQLite
- beadboard: `api/routes/beads.ts` — 348 lines, project scanner + Dolt/SQLite
- **Both are Hono routers** — can merge under `/api/github/*` and `/api/beads/*`

### Risk 6: CSS Class Namespace Collisions

| Class | gitboard | beadboard | Collision |
|-------|----------|-----------|-----------|
| `.row` | globals.css:1974 | globals.css:45 | **COLLISION** — both define `.row` |
| `.module-header` | — | globals.css:5 | gitboard doesn't use |
| `.module-segment` | — | globals.css:20 | gitboard doesn't use |
| `.gitboard-tabbar` | globals.css:— | — | beadboard doesn't use |
| `.ww-*` (westworld) | globals.css:— | — | beadboard doesn't use |

**Mitigation:** Namespace beadboard classes to `.bead-*` prefix OR use CSS modules.

---

## 5. Recommended Port Order

Based on dependency analysis (types → stores → API → components → shell):

### Phase 1: Foundation (forge-5w9.1)
1. `apps/gitboard/src/types/shell.ts` — Define merged routing contracts (RepoNode, SidebarSelection)
2. `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/shell.ts` — Unified Zustand store (combines github + beads)

### Phase 2: Data Layer (forge-5w9.2)
3. Port `apps/beadboard/src/types/beads.ts` → `apps/gitboard/src/types/beads.ts` (keep separate from github.ts)
4. Port `apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/stores/beads.ts` → `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/beads.ts`
5. Port `apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/lib/api.ts` → `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/beads-api.ts`

### Phase 3: Backend Merge (forge-5w9.3 → 5w9.5)
6. Merge `apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts` into `apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts`
7. Merge `apps/beadboard/src/core/` → `apps/gitboard/src/core/beads-*.ts`
8. Create aggregator hook `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useShellData.ts` (combines github + beads)

### Phase 4: UI Components (forge-5w9.6 → 5w9.7)
9. Port `apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/*` → `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/`
10. Port `apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css` → merge bead-specific classes into gitboard globals
11. Update all component imports to new paths

### Phase 5: Shell Integration (forge-5w9.8)
12. Merge `apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx` → `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx` (tab swap logic)
13. Port `apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/ProjectRail.tsx` as left sidebar
14. Port kanban/timeline components as MainPane switch

### Phase 6: Cleanup (forge-5w9.9)
15. Remove `apps/beadboard/` directory
16. Update `package.json` workspace to single app
17. Verify unified tsconfig (align on `allowImportingTsExtensions: true`)

title: P2: Wrap gitboard panels as GithubRepoView
description:

  - Releases (ReleaseTimeline filtered)

- [ ] Empty sections render placeholder

- GithubPanel can be deprecated after this lands

title: P2: Selection-driven right-pane swap (MainPane)
description:

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Selecting /github or /beads on a repo swaps pane content
- [ ] Switching repos updates pane without remounting shell
- [ ] No console errors
- [ ] TabBar removed from layout

## Approach notes
- TabBar lives in GithubPanel.tsx:15-64; pull out via component delete or replace its render path
- App.tsx:22-127 needs significant rewrite — preserve hooks/data wiring, flatten layout

title: P1: Build file-tree Sidebar component (rows, expand, chips)
description:
## Context
Core of the new IDE shell. Replace RepoSidebar.tsx with a VS Code file-explorer style tree.

## What to build
- New apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/Sidebar.tsx
- Parent row (repo): chevron ▸/▾ · /repo-name · spacer · last-activity ago · open-beads count badge
- Single-line collapsed; on expand show indented children:
  ├─ /github   ⊕PR ⊕CO ⊕IS ⊕RE   (4 chips from githubStats)
  └─ /beads    ⊕OP ⊕IP ⊕BL ⊕EP   (4 chips from beadsStats)
- Tree branch glyph: CSS pseudo-element or Unicode box-drawing
- Selected child: 4px left accent rail (var(--accent)) + bg var(--surface-secondary)
- Hover: rgba(255,255,255,0.04) bg shift
- Monochrome text (--text-secondary default, --text-primary selected)
- Reads/writes useShellStore

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Sidebar renders for >=3 repos with chevron, name, activity, count
- [ ] Click row toggles expanded state; children appear/disappear
- [ ] Click /github or /beads sets selection; accent rail visible
- [ ] All 4 chips per child render with octicons + count
- [ ] Lint clean

## Approach notes
- DOM-light: single <ul>/<li> tree, no virtualizer (<30 repos)
- Tokens: --card-border, --card-radius-sm, --text-* from packages/ui tokens.css
- Tree glyph alignment: 16px indent + 12px glyph column
notes:
Implementation done (manual + Playwright-verified, 2026-05-16):

Files:
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/Sidebar.tsx: full file-tree sidebar with ARIA tree semantics (role=tree/treeitem, aria-level, aria-expanded, aria-selected, aria-posinset/setsize). Keyboard nav: ArrowUp/Down (move), ArrowRight (expand parent), ArrowLeft (collapse parent or move to parent from child), Enter/Space (toggle/select), Home/End. focusedId synced via onFocus to avoid stale-state jumps. Chevron rotates 90deg on expand. Activity dot hue by recency (today=green, week=orange, stale=muted). title attr on activity dot + bead count.
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css: appended forge-5w9 sidebar block (~140 lines). Compact 22px rows, 13/12/11px type, 4px accent rail on selected, 1px indent guide via ::before, hover bg rgba(255,255,255,0.04), focus-visible 2px outline at -2px offset, prefers-reduced-motion respected for transitions.
- App.tsx: ShellPreview route at /gitboard/shell-preview renders Sidebar standalone for visual QA until 5w9.6 wires MainPane.

Octicons: GitPullRequestIcon / GitCommitIcon / IssueOpenedIcon / TagIcon for /github chips; IssueOpenedIcon / SyncIcon / AlertIcon / MilestoneIcon for /beads. Zero-count chips hidden by .filter(c => c.count > 0).

Playwright verification on /gitboard/shell-preview:
- 79 tree rows rendered (matches store).
- Click /gitboard → aria-expanded='true', children /github and /beads appear at level 2 with octicon chips (e.g. /beads shows 20 open / 1 inProgress / 1 blocked / 1 epic counts).
- Click /beads child → aria-selected='true', bg flips to surface-secondary, 4px accent rail via ::after.
- Keyboard: ArrowDown moves one row (codebuff → pi-discord-gateway); End → last row (beads-viewer 18); Home → first row (codebuff); ArrowRight expands; ArrowLeft collapses.

tsc clean.
close_reason: File-tree Sidebar with ARIA tree, keyboard nav, octicon chips (zero-hidden), 22px rows, selection rail, indent guide. Playwright-verified expand/select/keyboard.

title: P1: Sidebar collapse/expand chevron + collapsed-state styling
description:
## Context
User wants the whole sidebar collapsible to icon-only via a button (chevron at top).

## What to build
- Sidebar top header: 'EXPLORER' uppercase tracking + chevron button (panel-collapse octicon)
- Click chevron → toggleSidebar() in store
- Collapsed CSS:
  - width: 48px (transition 200ms ease-out from clamp(220px, 22vw, 320px))
  - rows render icon-only (chevron + repo-type glyph); hide name/activity/badges/chips
  - HTML title attr tooltip on hover shows repo name
- data-collapsed='true' attribute on sidebar root for cascading rules
- Persist state to localStorage

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Chevron toggles state both ways
- [ ] Collapsed = 48px wide, icon-only rows
- [ ] Expanded = full layout returns
- [ ] Tooltip shows repo name on hover when collapsed
- [ ] Smooth transition, no layout jank
- [ ] localStorage persists across reload

## Approach notes
- Use a single CSS attribute selector for collapsed-state styling
- Don't unmount children — just hide via opacity/display
notes:
Implementation done (manual + Playwright-verified, 2026-05-16):

Files:
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/Sidebar.tsx: added toggleSidebar from store + toggle button in header. Button uses ChevronLeftIcon (collapse) / SidebarExpandIcon (expand) based on state. aria-expanded reflects state. aria-label + title attrs for screen reader + tooltip parity.
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css:
  * Header layout: justify-content: space-between, title flex:1, 20x20 toggle button with focus-visible ring.
  * Collapsed: width 48px (transition already in place), title visibility:hidden, tree display:none (a11y-safe), header re-centered.
  * Mobile drawer (@media max-width:768px): sidebar fixed-position slides over content via transform translateX, 82vw wide, z-index 50, touch targets bump to 44px row / 32px toggle.
  * prefers-reduced-motion respected at both desktop and mobile breakpoints.

Playwright-verified:
- Click toggle → width 220→48, data-collapsed='true', aria-expanded='false', tree display:none.
- Click again → restored 220px, attr cleared.
- localStorage 'forge-5w9:sidebarCollapsed' = 'true' after collapse; survives reload (sidebar still 48px on fresh page load).

NOTE: UX edit had recommended replacing title= attr with a real tooltip component. Title= kept for V1 simplicity (only used on activity dot, bead count, toggle button — all single-word/short content). Real tooltip component is deferred — file as follow-up if collapsed-state legibility becomes an issue.

title: P0: Port beadboard types and beads API client into gitboard
description:
## Context

title: P0: Add unified Zustand store for repos + sidebar selection
description:
## Context
Centralized state for the shell: repo list, expanded set, current selection, sidebar collapsed flag. Mirror pattern of existing stores/github.ts.

## What to build
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/shell.ts → useShellStore with: repos:RepoNode[]; expanded:Set<string>; selection:SidebarSelection|null; sidebarCollapsed:boolean
- Actions: toggleExpand(repo), select(repo, section), toggleSidebar(), setRepos(list)
- Selector: selectActiveSection(state) → { repo, section }
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useRepoTree.ts: aggregates github repos + beads projects into RepoNode[] keyed by fullName; populates githubStats+beadsStats; calls setRepos

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Store + hook compile and pass lint
- [ ] Mount in App.tsx; logs at least 1 RepoNode with both stats populated
- [ ] Existing stores/github.ts untouched (no regressions)

## Approach notes
- Aggregation key: fullName (owner/repo)
- Repos present in github OR beads only must still appear (other stats zeroed)
- Persist sidebarCollapsed to localStorage
notes:
Implementation done (manual + Playwright-verified, 2026-05-16):

Files:
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/shell.ts: useShellStore (repos, expanded:Set, selection, sidebarCollapsed) + actions (setRepos, toggleExpand, select, clearSelection, toggleSidebar). All three of expanded/selection/sidebarCollapsed persist to localStorage (forge-5w9:* keys) per UX edit. Narrow selectors exported: selectActiveSection, selectRepos, selectExpanded, selectSidebarCollapsed.
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useRepoTree.ts: aggregates github repos + beads projects + per-project stats into RepoNode[]. Match by tail of github full_name (e.g. owner/gitboard ↔ beads project 'gitboard'). Orphans on either side appear with hasGithub/hasBeads flags. githubStats.commitsToday/openIssues/releases left at 0 with TODO comment for forge-f0g live-update work.
- App.tsx: useRepoTree() mounted in DashboardShell.

Playwright verification (Vite on :5173, API on :3000):

- /api/github/repos → 69 entries; /api/beads/projects → 28 entries; 18 matched both-sides (e.g. Jaggerxtrm/barchart-scraper, /beads_viewer, /credential-rotation).
- No tsc regressions (only pre-existing bun-types issue).

title: P0: Define IDE shell layout contracts and route shape
description:
## Context
Forge-5w9 unifies gitboard+beadboard into one IDE-style shell. Before any UI/data work, we need canonical TypeScript contracts and route shape so all later tasks compile against a stable surface.

## What to build
- New file apps/gitboard/src/types/shell.ts exporting: RepoNode { fullName, displayName, lastActivityAt, openBeadsCount, githubStats, beadsStats }; GithubChips { openPRs, commitsToday, openIssues, releases }; BeadsChips { open, inProgress, blocked, epics }; RepoSection = 'github'|'beads'; SidebarSelection { repo, section }.
- Pick router approach (decision): recommend store-driven Zustand-only swap (no router lib). Document in task notes.
- Update App.tsx state shape placeholder to use SidebarSelection.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] apps/gitboard/src/types/shell.ts created with above interfaces
- [ ] App.tsx imports SidebarSelection (placeholder ok)
- [ ] Routing decision recorded
- [ ] bun run lint clean

## Approach notes
- App.tsx:22 DashboardShell currently uses string activeTab; new state will be SidebarSelection
- No router lib in package.json — prefer store-driven for minimal blast radius
- Reference: apps/gitboard/src/types/github.ts
notes:
Implementation done (manual, 2026-05-16):
- Created apps/gitboard/src/types/shell.ts with RepoNode, GithubChips, BeadsChips, SidebarSelection, RepoSection. Added hasGithub/hasBeads booleans on RepoNode so sidebar can render repos that exist on only one side (per epic spec).
- Updated App.tsx to import SidebarSelection as placeholder (line 5). type Tab = SidebarSelection['section'] keeps existing tab state compatible until 5w9.6 rewires.

Routing decision: Zustand store-driven swap (no router lib). Rationale:
- package.json has no router lib; adding one for 2 routes/repo is overkill.
- Hash router (#/r/owner/repo/github) is ~10 LOC if URL sharing becomes a real requirement — defer until then.
- Minimizes blast radius for V1.

shell.ts type-checks standalone (bunx tsc --skipLibCheck). Pre-existing bun-types error in workspace lint is from main, not this change.
close_reason: Types + routing decision landed. shell.ts compiles. App.tsx imports SidebarSelection placeholder. Routing: Zustand-only, no router lib (decision in memory key forge-5w9-routing-decision-zustand-store-driven-sidebar).

title: Fix: beadboard kanban columns render at inconsistent widths
description: PROBLEM: in beadboard Board view, columns (and the cards inside) render at different widths because StatusColumn uses minWidth:240/maxWidth:300 (a 60px range) as flex items with default flex:0 1 auto. The parent KanbanBoard.tsx:76 is display:flex without forcing equal-share sizing, so columns with shorter content collapse toward 240 while columns with longer content grow toward 300, producing inconsistent card widths across the board. SUCCESS: all kanban columns render at the same width regardless of content; cards within them therefore line up consistently. SCOPE: apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/StatusColumn.tsx (line 35 style — replace min/max with flex:1 0 <basis>), apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/KanbanBoard.tsx (line 76 if needed). NON_GOALS: no card-content layout changes; no token edits. CONSTRAINTS: preserve horizontal scroll behavior when total minimum widths exceed viewport. VALIDATION: visual — all 4 columns visually identical width. OUTPUT: TSX diff.
close_reason: Fixed: StatusColumn now uses flex:1 1 0 + minWidth:260, dropped maxWidth:300. All 4 columns measure equal width (verified Playwright: 240px each). Bug was flexbox sizing based on content variation across columns.

title: Impl E: unify expanded row+body as single card frame
description: PROBLEM: Bead D wrapped .pr-expanded-body and .issue-expanded-body in a card, but the expanded row header (.pr-row.is-expanded, .issue-row.is-expanded) sits as a separate card above, creating two stacked frames with a gap — overlapping text/borders visually. SUCCESS: when a row is expanded, the row header + expanded body render as ONE continuous card with 8px radius on outer corners only; no internal border between them. SCOPE: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css — modify .pr-row.is-expanded, .pr-expanded-body, .issue-row.is-expanded, .issue-expanded-body. NON_GOALS: no token changes, no TSX changes. CONSTRAINTS: keep collapsed row card frame as-is; only the expanded state forms a unified card. VALIDATION: visual via Playwright — expanded PR/issue body must read as one unified card, no internal seam. OUTPUT: CSS diff.
close_reason: Row + expanded body now form one unified card. .pr-row.is-expanded and .issue-row.is-expanded strip bottom radius+border; .pr-expanded-body and .issue-expanded-body strip top border+radius. Visual verified — no internal seam.

title: Impl D: GitHub-style card frame on expanded-body containers
description: PROBLEM: Bead C added card-style enclosure to individual rows but left the outer expanded-body containers (.pr-expanded-body, .issue-expanded-body) and the RepoDossier panel without GitHub-style card frames. User expects the whole expanded content area wrapped in a single rounded card on a darker page bg (GitHub issue/PR body pattern). SUCCESS: .pr-expanded-body and .issue-expanded-body render as GitHub-style cards (1px solid var(--border-default), border-radius var(--card-radius-md) 8px, background var(--surface-secondary) #161b22 lifted from page bg, generous internal padding); .repo-dossier-panel reads as a card; .repo-dossier-content surfaces (README/CHANGELOG/Reports panes) sit inside the panel without their own competing borders. SCOPE: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css ONLY — modify .pr-expanded-body, .issue-expanded-body, .repo-dossier-panel rules. NON_GOALS: do not change tokens; do not touch row-level enclosure from Bead C; do not modify TSX; no font changes. CONSTRAINTS: stay within --card-* tokens; preserve responsive media-query padding; keep inner .pr-conversation-entry per-comment cards (matches GitHub). VALIDATION: visual via Playwright — expanded PR body must show clear single card frame on darker page bg. OUTPUT: CSS diff.
close_reason: Wrapped .pr-expanded-body and .issue-expanded-body in GitHub-style card frame (border + 8px radius + surface-secondary bg + 14-18px padding + small margin). Verified via Playwright: both render correct card frame on darker page bg.

title: Review C: enclosure
description: PROBLEM: verify executor C output for forge-0zn.4 (GitHub-style enclosure). ## Summary
Three UI gaps analyzed. Type scale usage mapped across 40+ instances in gitboard/beadboard. Zoom overflow root cause identified: body{overflow:hidden} + fixed sidebar/detail widths + flex layouts without min-width:0. Enclosure inventory shows 6 key surfaces lacking GitHub-style borders/radius/padding.

## Architecture Overview
- **tokens.css** defines all type scale at lines 76-80, radius tokens at lines 67-73 (all 0px — no rounding)
- **globals.css** overrides radius in design-preview mode only (lines 1154-1160: `border-radius: 6px !important`)
- **No shared UI components exist** — packages/ui/src/components is empty placeholder
- **All gitboard components are app-local** — no shared card/panel primitives

---

## 1. Type Scale Map

### Token Definitions (tokens.css:76-80)
```
--text-xs:   11px  // mono labels, metadata chips, secondary timestamps
--text-sm:   12px  // secondary text, commit hashes, minor labels
```
