## 2. Zoom Overflow Root Cause

### Offending Selectors

**Primary culprits** (globals.css):
```css
/* Line 44-46: Prevents browser scroll containment */
body {
  overflow: hidden;  /* Blocks viewport scroll on zoom */
}

/* tokens.css:86,89: Fixed pixel widths */
--sidebar-width: 220px;
--detail-width: 360px;
```

**Secondary — flex containers without shrink**:
- `App.tsx:26-30`: `<div className="westworld-app">` with `flexDirection: 'column'`, no `minWidth: 0`
- `globals.css:1008`: `.gitboard-main { overflow: hidden; }` — containment layer
- `globals.css:545`: `.fui-feed-column { overflow: hidden; }` — design-preview only
- `RepoSidebar.tsx:116-124`: `minWidth: width, maxWidth: width` — prevents column collapse

### Reproduction Steps
1. Set browser zoom to 150% (Ctrl+Plus 5x)
2. Open `/gitboard`
3. Observe horizontal scrollbar appears
4. Right panel content clips; sidebar remains fixed 220px
5. `minWidth: width` on sidebar prevents adaptive shrink

### Fix Pattern
```css
/* Replace fixed widths with clamp */
--sidebar-width: clamp(180px, 20vw, 280px);
--detail-width: clamp(280px, 30vw, 400px);

/* Add min-width to flex children */
.gitboard-main > div { min-width: 0; }

/* Remove body overflow override for zoom */
@media (min-resolution: 1.5dppx) {
  body { overflow-x: auto; }
}
```

**Files to modify**: `tokens.css:86,89`, `globals.css:44-46,1008`, `App.tsx:26-30`

---

```css
/* tokens.css additions */
```

title: Live Beads updates (forge-yhm.2: Dolt commit-hash poll + JSONL fallback + WS push + Zustand upsert)
description:
PROBLEM
Beadboard reflects bead changes only after a manual refresh OR after selectProject changes. Dolt is the canonical store (per metadata.json backend=dolt), JSONL is export/backup; specialist worktrees + remote pulls + direct edits all change state behind the dashboard's back. Need a live update path that respects source priority and survives Dolt-down fallback.

REFERENCES (READ, do not re-implement)
- apps/beadboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts (source priority ["dolt","sqlite","jsonl","unknown"]; reuse for source-health probe)
- apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts (existing /api/beads endpoints; reuse readers, do NOT bypass)
- apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts (existing Dolt read client; extend to poll commit hash)
- apps/beadboard/src/core/beads-reader.ts (existing JSONL reader; use for fallback)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/channels.ts (ChannelRegistry — ADD a new channel name "beads:changes"; same broadcast machinery)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/handler.ts (WsHandler — already routes channel subscriptions)
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/stores/beads.ts (useBeadsStore — ADD upsertIssue / removeIssue / moveToClosed / applyBatch)
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx (loadProjectData; will receive WS updates to keep store live)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts (wires poller + WS; this bead adds BeadsChangeWatcher startup)

SUCCESS
- New `BeadsChangeWatcher` (in apps/beadboard/src/core/) per tracked project:
  - **Source health probe** on startup + periodic re-check: `dolt-client.getCommitHash()` succeeds → Dolt healthy; otherwise mark project source as "jsonl"/"sqlite" fallback.
  - **Primary signal — Dolt commit-hash poll**: query Dolt every 2s for active project (= project currently selected by any client), every 10s for others. On hash change, diff via `getIssues(projectId, { since: lastSeenUpdated })` OR refetch the changed-set and emit upsert events.
  - **Fallback signal — fs.watch on .beads/issues.jsonl** for each project: debounce 1s + hash mtime; on change emit `{kind: "beads:batch", source: "jsonl"}` events with the loaded delta.
  - **Reverse import gap**: if JSONL changes but Dolt commit hash hasn't changed, emit a `source: "jsonl"` event AND flag the project as "drift detected" in source-health payload — do NOT silently overwrite Dolt assumptions.
- New WS channel `beads:changes` on existing ChannelRegistry. Payload envelope:
  ```ts
  {
    event: "beads:upsert" | "beads:close" | "beads:delete" | "beads:batch" | "beads:sync_hint" | "beads:source_health",
    projectId: string,
    source: "dolt" | "jsonl" | "sqlite",
    version: string, // dolt commit hash or jsonl mtime
    data: { issueId?, issue?, ids?, health? }
  }
  ```
- Server-side coalesce 1500ms; flush at 50 changes; >50 emits `beads:sync_hint` for client to refetch project list once.
- Client (App.tsx + new hook `useBeadsLive`) subscribes to `beads:changes` filtered by current `selectedProjectId`. Routes events to new Zustand actions:
  - `upsertIssue(issue)` — replace by id in `issues[]` or prepend if new.
  - `moveToClosed(issueId)` — remove from `issues`, prepend into `closedIssues`.
  - `removeIssue(issueId)` — remove from both arrays.
  - `applyBatch(changes)` — atomic apply with single re-render.
  - On `sync_hint` — call existing `loadProjectData(projectId)`.
  - On `source_health` — store `{[projectId]: health}` in store for badge display.
- Optional: small "source-health" pill in beadboard topbar showing current source (Dolt ✓ / JSONL fallback / Dolt drift).

SCOPE
- NEW: apps/beadboard/src/core/beads-change-watcher.ts (BeadsChangeWatcher class)
- MODIFY: apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts (add getCommitHash + fetchSince helpers; do NOT change existing readers)
- MODIFY: apps/beadboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts (expose health-probe helper; do NOT change scan API)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts (start BeadsChangeWatcher with the registry)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/channels.ts (verify channel registration; "beads:changes" is just a new subscribe key)
- MODIFY: apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/stores/beads.ts (add upsertIssue / moveToClosed / removeIssue / applyBatch / setSourceHealth)
- NEW: apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useBeadsLive.ts (mirror of useWebSocket usage in gitboard's useGithubActivity)
- MODIFY: apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx (use useBeadsLive; mount source-health pill)
- NEW: apps/beadboard/tests/core/beads-change-watcher.test.ts (hash poll detects change, fs.watch fallback emits, drift detection)
- NEW: apps/beadboard/tests/dashboard/stores/beads-upsert.test.ts (upsert/move/remove preserve order)

IMPERATIVE REUSE RULES (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
- USE existing ChannelRegistry + WsHandler; do NOT create a new WS server or transport.
- USE existing dolt-client read paths; do NOT add a parallel SQL client.
- USE existing beads-reader for JSONL fallback; do NOT re-implement JSONL parsing.
- EXTEND useBeadsStore; do NOT create a second store.
- Source priority is fixed: ["dolt", "sqlite", "jsonl"] per project-scanner; do NOT change ordering.
- fs.watch via Node fs (we are on Bun which supports fs.watch) — do NOT pull chokidar.
- Coalesce batches in-memory; do NOT persist queue to disk.
- TypeScript strict mode.

NON_GOALS
- No GitHub PR/issue live updates (that is forge-yhm.1's scope).
- No Dolt CDC/binlog integration.
- No bd CLI hook (poor coverage; explicit non-goal).
- No "edit issue from UI" mutations.
- No persistence of source-health history.
- No multi-tenant project filtering at WS layer beyond simple `projectId` match in payload.

CONSTRAINTS
- Bun runtime; reuse Bun.spawnSync / bun:sqlite where existing readers do.
- All Dolt queries via existing dolt-client; if a helper is missing, ADD it there.
- fs.watch must handle ENOENT (project deleted) gracefully — log + stop watcher, do not throw.
- Debounce config: hardcoded 1500ms batch; 1s fs.watch debounce.
- Active-project cadence: 2s commit-hash poll; idle: 10s; configurable via constants at top of watcher file.
- WS payloads <16KB; overflow → `sync_hint`.
- No race: emit `version` (commit hash or mtime) so client can compare and ignore stale.

VALIDATION
- `bun run --filter @xtrm/beadboard test` green (existing + 2 new).
- `bun run --filter @xtrm/beadboard build:dashboard` green.
- Smoke (orchestrator runs):
  1. Open beadboard, select a Dolt-backed project.
  2. Run `bd update <id> --notes "..."` in another terminal.
  3. Within ~3s the dashboard's issue list reflects the new notes without refresh.
  4. Run `bd close <id>` → issue moves from `issues` to `closedIssues` lane.
  5. Stop the Dolt server; verify source-health pill flips to "JSONL fallback"; edit JSONL directly; UI updates with `source: "jsonl"`.

OUTPUT
- File diff list
- WS payload type definitions
- Source-health state machine diagram (text)
- Residual risks (hash-poll latency, fs.watch rename race, multi-client coalesce interactions)
notes:
FORGE-DGD + FORGE-F0G LANDED. Conform to:

- Use RealtimeEnvelope<E, D> from apps/gitboard/src/types/realtime.ts; version field is STRING (Dolt commit hash for beads events).
- Channel: 'beads:changes'. Subscribe filters by projectId at client; server emits global.
- Events per BeadsRealtimeEvent union in types/realtime.ts:
  beads:issue.upsert | beads:issue.close | beads:issue.delete |
  beads:issue.deferred | beads:issue.superseded | beads:issue.flagged | beads:issue.unflagged |
  beads:dep.upsert | beads:dep.delete |
  beads:memory.upsert | beads:memory.delete |
  beads:kv.upsert | beads:kv.delete |
  beads:comment.append | beads:batch | beads:sync_hint | beads:source_health
- Add per-event payload type aliases to types/realtime.ts BEFORE producer wiring (BeadsIssueUpsert, BeadsDepUpsert, BeadsMemoryUpsert, BeadsKvUpsert, BeadsSourceHealth, BeadsBatch, etc.).
- Watcher diffs FOUR entity snapshots per Dolt commit-hash tick: issues, deps, memories, kv. Emit alias events (deferred/superseded/flagged) when prior→current field transitions match per the design.
- Publish via ChannelRegistry.publish('beads:changes', '<event>', data, version=doltCommitHash) — registry stamps seq/ts/boot_id automatically.
- JSONL fallback fs.watch is for issues.jsonl ONLY; memories/kv/deps degrade to stale + flagged via beads:source_health { source:'jsonl', drift:true, scope:'memories'|'kv'|'deps' }.
- Coalesce 1500ms / 50 items / 16KB → overflow emits beads:sync_hint.
- Client Zustand actions in beadboard (or wherever beads state lives now): upsertIssue, closeIssue, removeIssue, upsertDep, removeDep, upsertMemory, removeMemory, upsertKv, removeKv, applyBatch, applySyncHint(projectId), setSourceHealth.
- IMPORTANT: do NOT use Bun.sleep() in code paths touched by Node-environment tests; use await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, n)) instead (lesson from forge-f0g).

title: Live GitHub PR/Issue updates (forge-yhm.1: server poll + WS push + client upsert)
description:
PROBLEM
Today's GitHub poller only polls /users/<me>/events, which misses issues + PRs created by collaborators. forge-1kv (just shipped) and recent collaborator activity (Rico1109 #257) only surface after a manual one-shot backfill. Dashboard cannot reflect remote changes without page refresh.

REFERENCES (READ, do not re-implement)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts (existing GithubPoller class; pollUser, backfillPrsAndIssues, setInterval at line 595 — extend, don't fork)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/github-store.ts (insertPr/insertIssue/upsertRepo — use existing helpers)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/channels.ts (ChannelRegistry — already broadcasts on "github:activity")
- apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/handler.ts (WsHandler connection lifecycle)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.ts (existing useWebSocket subscription; wsHandler handles "new_event"/"new_commits" — add "new_pr"/"new_issue")
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/github.ts (Zustand store; add upsertPr / upsertIssue / markRepoUnread is already there)
- apps/gitboard/src/types/github.ts (GithubPr, GithubIssue shapes)

SUCCESS
- New `RepoPollerLoop` class (or extension on GithubPoller) periodically fetches `/repos/:owner/:repo/issues?state=all&since=<lastIssueUpdated>&per_page=100` and `/repos/:owner/:repo/pulls?state=all&sort=updated&direction=desc&per_page=100` per tracked repo.
- ETag cache per (repo, endpoint): send `If-None-Match`, skip parse on 304.
- Watermark tracking: per repo store `last_issue_updated_at` and `last_pr_updated_at`; stop iterating pages once a row predates the watermark.
- Adaptive cadence:
  - **1 min** for "active" repos = (repo has push event in last 1h) OR (repo currently in `filter.repos` from any connected client) OR (manually toggled active in UI later)
  - 10 min default
  - 30 min for repos with no activity in 24h
  - Backoff: pause polling for the next minute when `X-RateLimit-Remaining < 500`; resume below 80% budget consumed
- Detection: when an inserted PR or issue is NEW (insert returned true) OR has updated since cached version, build a normalized payload and broadcast on `github:activity` channel: `{ event: "new_pr" | "new_issue", data: { repo, number, title, state, author, url, updated_at } }`
- Client wsHandler in useGithubActivity adds two cases:
  - `new_pr`: call `useGithubStore.getState().upsertPr(pr)` and `markRepoUnread(pr.repo)`
  - `new_issue`: call `useGithubStore.getState().upsertIssue(issue)` and `markRepoUnread(issue.repo)`
- Coalesce: server-side buffer events 1500ms; if buffer >50 items, emit a single `{ event: "sync_hint", repos: [...] }` payload that causes client to refetch the list once instead of upserting individually.
- New Zustand actions: `upsertPr(pr)`, `upsertIssue(issue)` — replace existing element by `(repo, number)` key or prepend if new; preserve current sort.

SCOPE
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts (add RepoPollerLoop, ETag cache, watermarks, adaptive cadence, broadcast on insert)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/core/github-store.ts (add `last_issue_updated_at` / `last_pr_updated_at` to repo row, OR a side table `repo_poll_state`; expose helper getters/setters)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts (wire poller's broadcast → registry channel "github:activity")
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/channels.ts (broadcasts already exist; verify; no API change unless event type union widens)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/github.ts (add upsertPr, upsertIssue actions, sync_hint handler)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.ts (handle new_pr / new_issue / sync_hint in wsHandler)
- NEW: apps/gitboard/tests/core/github-poller-loop.test.ts (smoke: poll cycle inserts new PR, broadcasts payload; ETag 304 path; rate-limit backoff)
- NEW: apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/stores/github-upsert.test.ts (upsert by composite key, preserve order)

IMPERATIVE REUSE RULES (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
- EXTEND existing GithubPoller class; do NOT create a parallel poller class file.
- USE existing ChannelRegistry + WsHandler; do NOT add a new WS channel or transport.
- USE existing `insertPr` / `insertIssue` helpers; do NOT bypass and write SQL directly elsewhere.
- USE existing `useGithubStore` + `useWebSocket` hooks; do NOT create parallel state or a second WS client.
- ETag stored in-memory Map at first; add DB persistence ONLY if scope explicitly says so (it doesn't).
- Cadence "active" definition is described above — do NOT add a config UI or env var yet (hardcoded thresholds OK).
- No external libraries (gh REST via existing `githubApi` helper).

NON_GOALS
- No beads change stream (forge-yhm.2 covers that).
- No issue/PR detail re-poll for selected items (separate concern; mention in OUTPUT as residual).
- No `/console` integration.
- No persistence of ETag/watermark across server restarts (in-memory OK).
- No GraphQL migration.
- No UI controls for cadence (hardcoded thresholds).

CONSTRAINTS
- TypeScript strict mode.
- Bun runtime (`Bun.spawnSync`, `bun:sqlite`).
- Hono for routes (no change).
- Reuse `getGithubToken()` from poller; never read env directly elsewhere.
- All HTTP requests must check `X-RateLimit-Remaining` and short-circuit polling if <500.
- WS payload size <16KB per message; batches that exceed should split into `sync_hint`.
- Maintain backward compatibility with existing "new_event" and "new_commits" payloads — they keep working.

VALIDATION
- `bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard test` green (existing 208 + 2 new = 210).
- `bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard build:dashboard` green.
- Smoke (orchestrator runs):
  1. Open an issue on `Jaggerxtrm/xtrm-tools` via `gh issue create` after server is running.
  2. Within ~1 min the dashboard's Issues tab shows it without page refresh.
  3. Open a PR similarly; PR row appears in PR tab.
  4. Observe `X-RateLimit-Remaining` in server logs; confirm adaptive cadence.

OUTPUT
- File diff list (estimate 6 modified + 2 new tests)
- New WS event payload shapes (typed)
- Adaptive cadence thresholds with rationale
- Residual risks (e.g. detail refetch not implemented, ETag not persisted)
notes:
FORGE-DGD CONTRACT LANDED (commit 8d24ba7). This bead must conform:

- Use RealtimeEnvelope<E, D> from apps/gitboard/src/types/realtime.ts; do NOT invent payload shapes.
- Event names are 'github:pr.upsert', 'github:issue.upsert', 'github:event.append', 'github:release.upsert', 'github:sync_hint', 'github:rate_limit' (per GithubRealtimeEvent union). Replace draft 'new_pr'/'new_issue' wording.
- Publish via ChannelRegistry.publish(channel, event, data) on 'github:activity' channel — the registry stamps seq/ts/version/boot_id automatically.
- Pass 'version' arg to publish as the row's updated_at ISO (use it for dedupe in upsert actions).
- Legacy 'new_event' / 'new_commits' handlers in dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.ts must be RENAMED (not aliased) to the new event names — there is only one consumer.
- Per-event payload types must be added to types/realtime.ts (GithubPrUpsert = GithubPr, GithubIssueUpsert = GithubIssue, etc.) BEFORE the producer wiring.
- Server-side coalesce 1500ms / 50 items / 16KB → overflow emits github:sync_hint with reason: 'overflow' | 'rate_limit'.
- Adaptive cadence + ETag + watermark logic unchanged from original scope.

description:
PROBLEM
Currently the dashboard only learns about new GitHub PRs/Issues via the poller's initial backfill (one-shot at startup), and never reflects beads changes between manual refreshes. User wants live updates covering BOTH the GitHub side AND the beads side. Beads has two sources: Dolt server (canonical, sometimes drifts) and .beads/issues.jsonl (backup). Need to pick the right canonical change-source.

QUESTIONS (please answer ALL with reasoning + concrete recommendation)

1. **Beads source-of-truth**: Is Dolt the true canonical, with JSONL the backup? When/how do they diverge? If a session edits JSONL directly (e.g. specialist worktree), is that reflected back to Dolt?

2. **Change detection — beads side**:
   a. Dolt SQL polling against `commit_hash`/`commit_log` for change deltas?
   b. Dolt replication (binlog/CDC) — does Dolt expose one?
   c. fs.watch on .beads/issues.jsonl per project (cheap, lossy on rename)?
   d. Hook the `bd` CLI to emit events?
   Rank with tradeoffs.

3. **Change detection — GitHub side**: Currently poller hits user-event feed only. To catch issues opened by collaborators we need per-repo /issues + /prs polling on a separate cadence. What's the right interval given GH REST rate-limit (5000/hr)? Use ETag/conditional requests to save quota?

4. **WS channel architecture**: One unified `forge:live` channel with typed payloads `{kind: "github:pr" | "github:issue" | "beads:issue" | "beads:close"}`, OR separate channels per source (`github:activity` + `beads:changes`)? Tradeoffs for routing, subscription complexity, multi-project filtering.

5. **Granularity & cadence**: Push every row mutation immediately, or coalesce in 1–2s batches? What if 50 beads close at once from `xt end`?

6. **Client mutation strategy**: When `new_issue` arrives, do we (a) prepend to store and re-render, (b) refetch the whole issue list, (c) refetch only that one issue's detail? Same question for beads — Zustand `useBeadsStore` currently holds whole arrays.

7. **forge-yhm scope split**: Should we ship this as one unified bead, OR split into:
   - forge-yhm.1 GH periodic poll + WS push (smaller, well-understood)
   - forge-yhm.2 Beads change stream (riskier, dolt-dependent)
   Recommend the split with rationale.

8. **Premortem**: 30 days after shipping, what kills this design? Rate-limit exhaustion? WS reconnect storms? Stale Dolt port? Race conditions between push and poll? List the top 3 failure modes and the cheapest mitigations.

OUTPUT
- Numbered answers to all 8 with explicit recommendation per question
- Final architectural recommendation in 5 lines max
- Risks ranked

notes:
### Specialist Output — overthinker (job 7f4eed) [WAITING — more output may follow]

## Summary

Read repo evidence. No file changes. No bd claim/close possible: no bash tool exposed. GitNexus/Serena tools unavailable in harness, so used read/grep/ls only.

## Phase 1 - Initial Analysis

Goal: live dashboard updates for GitHub + beads.

Local facts:
- GitHub poller exists in `apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts`.
- Current loop polls only `/users/:username/events` every 5 min.
- PR/issues full backfill runs only inside `backfill()`, not periodic.
- WS infra exists: `github:activity` channel in `apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/channels.ts`.
- Client already subscribes to `github:activity` in `useGithubActivity.ts`.
- Beads API reads sources by priority: Dolt → SQLite → JSONL in `apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts`.
- Scanner reports same priority in `apps/beadboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts`.
- `.beads/export-state.json` stores `last_dolt_commit`, issue count, timestamp.
- `.beads/README.md` says Beads stored in Dolt, syncs with commits, JSONL present as export/backup.

Core problem: need change producer, event envelope, client merge path.

## Phase 2 - Devil's Advocate

Assumption: Dolt always canonical. Risk: local Dolt server stale/down/wrong DB. Evidence: `.beads/dolt-server.log` has many `table not found` errors. Need fallback health.

Assumption: JSONL watch catches all. Risk: Dolt writes may not export JSONL immediately; fs.watch misses rename/burst; worktrees skipped by scanner.

Assumption: GitHub events enough. False. User event feed misses collaborator-created issues/PRs unless authenticated user involved.

Assumption: push every change. Risk: `xt end` closes many beads; WS flood + React re-render storm.

Assumption: client can mutate arrays. Risk: filters/sorting/source transitions make prepend wrong. Need idempotent upsert + periodic reconciliation.

## Phase 3 - Synthesis

Best architecture: one live bus, two producers.

- GitHub producer: keep user events poll. Add per-tracked-repo issue/PR updater with watermarks + ETag.
- Beads producer: poll canonical Dolt commit hash when healthy. Use JSONL watch as fallback/supplement. Do not rely on CLI hook first.
- WS: unified `forge:live` channel, typed payloads, project/repo filters.
- Server coalesces changes in 1–2s batches.
- Client applies cheap idempotent upserts for visible rows, refetches detail when selected row affected, full list reconciliation on reconnect/source drift.

## Phase 4 - Final Refined Output

## Final Answer

### 1. Beads source-of-truth

**Recommendation: Treat Dolt as canonical when healthy; JSONL as backup/export; SQLite as middle fallback.**

Reason:
- `ProjectScanner` priority: `["dolt", "sqlite", "jsonl", "unknown"]`.
- `beadsRoutes` reads sources in same priority.
- `.beads/README.md` says issues are “Stored in Dolt database”.
- `.beads/export-state.json` records `last_dolt_commit`, timestamp, count. Looks export marker, not primary DB.

Divergence:
- Dolt server down/stale/wrong DB.
- JSONL export lag after Dolt mutation.
- Worktree/specialist edits touching JSONL directly.
- Git hook sync not run yet.
- Manual file edits bypass `bd`.

Direct JSONL edit reflected to Dolt? **No evidence in repo. Assume no automatic reverse import.** Needs `bd` import/sync path or explicit CLI action. Direct JSONL writes should be treated as divergent backup state, not canonical mutation.

---

### 2. Change detection — beads side

**Recommendation rank:**

1. **Dolt SQL polling against commit hash / log**
   - Best canonical signal.
   - Cheap: poll `HEAD`/commit hash every 1–2s per active project, 5–15s idle.
   - On hash change, query updated issues by `updated_at`/diff if available.
   - Needs schema probe because local code has no commit-log implementation yet.

2. **fs.watch on `.beads/issues.jsonl`**
   - Good fallback and wake-up signal.
   - Cheap, easy.
   - Lossy on atomic rename/burst. Must debounce then refetch.
   - Useful when Dolt unreachable or JSONL-only project.

3. **Hook `bd` CLI to emit events**
   - Precise for local `bd` commands.
   - Bad coverage: misses direct DB changes, remote pulls, specialists without wrapper, manual edits.
   - Good later as optimization, not foundation.

4. **Dolt replication/binlog/CDC**
   - Do not pick now.
   - No local evidence of exposed CDC path.
   - Highest complexity.

Concrete beads design:
- `BeadsChangeWatcher`
  - source health probe.
  - if Dolt healthy: poll commit hash.
  - if hash changed: refetch changed issue summaries/details or list.
  - always watch JSONL as fallback trigger.
  - debounce 1–2s, publish batch.

---

### 3. Change detection — GitHub side

**Recommendation: Add per-repo polling on separate cadence: 10 min default, staggered, ETag + watermarks.**

Current:
- `/users/:username/events` every 5 min.
- Backfill PR/issues one-shot only.

Need:
- For each tracked repo:
  - `/repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues?state=all&since=<last_issue_updated>&per_page=100`
  - `/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls?state=all&sort=updated&direction=desc&per_page=100`
  - stop when `updated_at <= watermark`.

Rate math:
- 2 requests/repo/cycle.
- 10 min cadence = 12 requests/repo/hour.
- 50 repos = 600/hour.
- 100 repos = 1200/hour.
- Under 5000/hour with room for detail calls.
- 5 min cadence doubles cost: 100 repos = 2400/hour, still possible but less safe.

Use ETag:
- Store ETag per URL+params.
- Send `If-None-Match`.
- On 304, skip parsing/upsert.
- Still implement hard budget using `X-RateLimit-Remaining`.

Adaptive cadence:
- Active repo: 2–5 min.
- Normal: 10 min.
- Idle/no changes 24h: 30 min.
- Low rate remaining: backoff to 30–60 min.

---

### 4. WS channel architecture

**Recommendation: one unified `forge:live` channel with typed payloads.**

Payload:
```ts
{
  kind: "github:pr" | "github:issue" | "github:event" | "beads:issue" | "beads:close",
  projectId?: string,
  repo?: string,
  op: "upsert" | "delete" | "close" | "batch" | "sync_hint",
  data: unknown,
  version?: string,
  source?: "dolt" | "sqlite" | "jsonl" | "github"
}
```

Why unified:
- One client subscription.
- One reconnect/backoff path.
- One batch envelope.
- Easy multi-source dashboard.

Separate channels downside:
- `github:activity` + `beads:changes` duplicates reconnect, filter, handler code.
- Cross-source views need multiple subscriptions.

Caveat:
- Keep internal producer names separate.
- Channel can support filters: projectId/repo/kinds during subscribe later.

---

### 5. Granularity & cadence

**Recommendation: coalesce 1–2s batches, never push every row immediately by default.**

Why:
- `xt end` can close 50 beads.
- React/Zustand full-array writes can rerender hard.
- fs.watch may emit burst events.

Batch shape:
```ts
{
  kind: "beads:batch",
  projectId,
  changes: [
    { op: "upsert", issueId, status, updated_at },
    { op: "close", issueId, closed_at }
  ],
  sourceVersion: "dolt-commit-hash"
}
```

Rules:
- If one change: still send single or batch; client handler same.
- Max batch delay: 2s.
- Max batch size: e.g. 100; overflow sends `sync_hint` causing refetch.
- On source version jump with unknown diff: send `sync_hint`.

---

### 6. Client mutation strategy

**Recommendation: hybrid. Upsert list rows, refetch selected detail, full refetch on uncertain batch/reconnect.**

GitHub:
- `new_event`: prepend/upsert existing `events`, already supported by `prependEvent`.
- `github:pr` / `github:issue`: add store actions `upsertPr`, `upsertIssue`.
- If selected PR/issue changed: refetch detail endpoint.
- Do not full-refetch list for every event.

Beads:
- Add `upsertIssue`, `removeIssue`, `moveToClosed`, `applyBatch` to `useBeadsStore`.
- If issue changes status from open → closed: remove from `issues`, upsert into `closedIssues`.
- If selected issue changed: refetch `/api/beads/projects/:id/issues/:issueId`.
- If `sourceVersion` gap, overflow, reconnect: refetch whole visible project lists.

Reason:
- Current store holds whole arrays; full list refetch per mutation is safe but wasteful.
- Pure local mutation risks filter/sort drift.
- Hybrid gives responsive UI + eventual consistency.

---

### 7. forge-yhm scope split

**Recommendation: split.**

- `forge-yhm.1`: GitHub periodic repo PR/issues poll + WS push.
- `forge-yhm.2`: Beads change stream + source-health + Dolt/JSONL fallback.

Rationale:
- GH path is bounded, existing DB/store/routes already support PR/issues.
- Beads path has source-truth risk, Dolt health, JSONL drift, debounce, worktree questions.
- Split gives usable live GitHub sooner.
- Split lets beads watcher ship after schema/source probing.
- Review/test scope smaller.

---

### 8. Premortem — top 3 failure modes

#### 1. Rate-limit exhaustion
Cause:
- Too many repos.
- Detail endpoint fanout.
- No ETag/backoff.

Cheap mitigation:
- 10 min default cadence.
- ETag cache.
- Watermarks.
- Repo staggering.
- Stop polling when `X-RateLimit-Remaining < 500`.
- UI source-health badge.

#### 2. Beads source drift / stale Dolt port
Cause:
- Dolt server points to wrong DB.
- JSONL updated but Dolt stale.
- Direct JSONL edits not imported.

Cheap mitigation:
- Source health model visible in payload/UI.
- Compare Dolt count/hash vs JSONL mtime/export-state.
- Fallback to JSONL only when Dolt unhealthy.
- Emit `source: "dolt" | "jsonl"` in WS payload.
- Periodic full reconciliation.

#### 3. WS reconnect/batch race
Cause:
- Client disconnects during batch.
- Poll writes DB then WS push lost.
- Client applies stale event after refetch.

Cheap mitigation:
- Every payload carries `sourceVersion` + `updated_at`.
- Client idempotent upsert by id and newer timestamp.
- On reconnect: full refetch visible lists.
- Exponential backoff already exists; add jitter.
- Server sends `sync_hint` after overflow/gap.

---

## Risks

1. **Dolt watcher uncertainty** — local code has no commit-log probe yet. Need schema/research pass.
2. **JSONL reverse-sync assumption** — direct JSONL edits likely not reflected to Dolt.
3. **GitHub polling cost** — safe with ETag/watermarks, risky without.
4. **Client store churn** — needs upsert actions to avoid full-array thrash.
5. **WS event loss** — fix by reconnect refetch + sourceVersion.

## Verification

Evidence read:
- `apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/channels.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/github.ts`
- `apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts`
- `apps/beadboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts`
- `apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts`
- `.beads/README.md`
- `.beads/export-state.json`

## Beads

Could not run:
- `bd update forge-7ff --claim`
- `bd close forge-7ff --reason="done"`

Reason: no bash tool exposed in current harness.

```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "summary": "Completed 4-phase Overthinker analysis for forge-yhm live updates. Recommended split delivery, Dolt-first beads watcher with JSONL fallback, repo-level GitHub polling with ETag, unified forge:live WS channel, batched payloads, hybrid client mutation strategy.",
  "files_changed": [],
  "follow_ups": [
    "Run Dolt schema probe for commit hash/log tables before implementing beads watcher.",
    "Confirm bd JSONL direct-edit reverse-sync behavior with Beads docs or CLI source.",
    "Implement forge-yhm.1 before forge-yhm.2."
  ],
  "risks": [
    "Dolt commit/change APIs not verified locally.",
    "Direct JSONL edits may diverge from Dolt.",
    "GitHub rate limits if per-repo polling lacks ETag/watermarks/backoff."
  ],
  "verification": [
    "Read GitHub poller, WS channel/client, GitHub store hook.",
```

notes:
### Specialist Output — overthinker (job 2bee39) [WAITING — more output may follow]

## Summary

forge-yhm should split. GH live path small. Beads stream risky because Dolt/JSONL truth split needs source contract.

## Status

Read-only scope complete. No files changed. `bd` claim/close not run: no bash tool available in harness.

## Phase 1 — Initial Analysis

### 1. Beads source-of-truth

**Recommendation: Dolt canonical, JSONL backup/export.**

Evidence:
- `.beads/metadata.json` says `"backend": "dolt"`, `"dolt_mode": "server"`.
- `.beads/export-state.json` records `last_dolt_commit`.
- Beadboard source priority already says `["dolt", "sqlite", "jsonl", "unknown"]`.
- Docs/spec in repo references Dolt > SQLite > JSONL priority.

Divergence cases:
- Dolt server unavailable/stale port → UI falls back to SQLite/JSONL.
- JSONL export lags Dolt commit.
- Specialist worktree edits `.beads/issues.jsonl` directly → not reflected in Dolt unless explicit import/reconcile exists.
- Current scanner skips `.worktrees`, so worktree JSONL edits may be invisible.

**Concrete recommendation:** Treat Dolt as canonical when reachable and healthy. Treat JSONL as read-only fallback plus drift signal. Do not assume JSONL direct edits sync back to Dolt.

---

### 2. Change detection — beads side

**Ranking:**

1. **Dolt SQL polling on commit/log state**
   - Best canonical signal.
   - Poll `dolt_log`/current commit hash or equivalent. On change, diff issue rows or refetch changed IDs.
   - Also check uncommitted working set if `bd` can mutate without commit.
   - Risk: schema/table names need live Dolt validation.

2. **fs.watch on `.beads/issues.jsonl`**
   - Cheap fallback.
   - Catches backup export and direct JSONL edits.
   - Lossy on rename/atomic replace; use debounce + stat/hash reread.
   - Watch main repo and known worktree beads paths if scanner supports them.

3. **Hook `bd` CLI to emit events**
   - Great latency.
   - Bad coverage: misses direct Dolt SQL, JSONL edits, old bd versions, specialist tool bypass.
   - Better future optimization, not canonical detector.

4. **Dolt binlog/CDC**
   - Do not ship first.
   - Dolt CDC support unclear from local repo evidence.
   - Operational burden high vs polling.

**Concrete recommendation:** Implement hybrid: Dolt commit polling primary, JSONL watcher fallback/drift detector. Leave bd event hook optional later.

---

### 3. Change detection — GitHub side

Current poller uses user events, so collaborator-created issues can be missed.

**Recommendation: add per-repo polling:**
- `/repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues?state=all&since=<lastSeenUpdatedAt>&per_page=100`
- `/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls?state=all&sort=updated&direction=desc&per_page=100`

Cadence:
- Default **5 min** for normal tracked repo count.
- Formula: `repos * endpoints * 12 req/hr` at 5 min.
  - 50 repos → 1,200 req/hr.
  - 100 repos → 2,400 req/hr.
  - 200 repos → 4,800 req/hr, too close.
- Use dynamic cadence:
  - ≤100 repos: 5 min.
  - 101–200 repos: 10 min.
  - >200 repos: 15–30 min or active repos only.

ETag:
- Use `If-None-Match`.
- Store ETag per `(repo, endpoint, query)`.
- 304 responses save payload and should reduce primary rate pressure.
- Still enforce local budget because detail calls and retries cost.

**Concrete recommendation:** Budget to 60% of 5000/hr max, jitter polls, back off below 500 remaining.

---

### 4. WS channel architecture

**Recommendation: separate channels, shared envelope.**

Use:
- `github:activity`
- `beads:changes`

Payload envelope:
```ts
{
  kind: "github:pr" | "github:issue" | "beads:issue" | "beads:close",
  projectId?: string,
  repo?: string,
  id: string,
  action: "created" | "updated" | "closed" | "deleted",
  data?: unknown,
  changedAt: string
}
```

Tradeoffs:
- Separate channels reduce unnecessary subscriptions.
- Beadboard need not receive GitHub traffic.
- Gitboard need not receive Beads traffic.
- Multi-project filtering stays payload-level.
- Unified `forge:live` easier for console shell later, but noisy now.

**Concrete recommendation:** Ship separate channels now. Add `forge:live` bridge only when console needs one combined feed.

---

### 5. Granularity & cadence

**Recommendation: coalesce 1–2s batches.**

Why:
- `xt end` can close 50 beads.
- Per-row WS spam causes render storms.
- Batches let client dedupe and refetch once.

Batch shape:
```ts
{
  kind: "beads:batch",
  projectId: "...",
  changes: [
    { kind: "beads:close", id: "forge-123", action: "closed" }
  ],
  highWatermark: "doltCommitOrJsonlHash"
}
```

Policy:
- Debounce 1000ms.
- Flush at 100 changes.
- Flush immediately for manual single action if no batch pending.
- Include watermark to avoid stale push vs poll races.

---

### 6. Client mutation strategy

**Recommendation: upsert row, refetch detail only when needed, full refetch only for large/ambiguous batches.**

GitHub:
- `github:pr` / `github:issue` with full row → upsert into `prs`/`issues`.
- Mark repo unread.
- Async refetch repo stats/summary.
- If selected item matches → refetch one detail endpoint.

Beads:
- Add Zustand helpers: `upsertIssue`, `removeIssue`, `upsertClosedIssue`, `applyIssueBatch`.
- If payload has full row → mutate arrays.
- If close event only has ID → move/remove then fetch issue detail.
- If batch > 25 or watermark gap → refetch current project issue list + closed list once.

Avoid:
- Full list refetch per event.
- Blind prepend without dedupe/filter checks.

---

### 7. forge-yhm scope split

**Recommendation: split.**

- `forge-yhm.1` — GH repo poll + WS push.
  - Known API.
  - Existing DB/store supports PRs/issues.
  - Existing WS channel exists.
  - Lower risk.

- `forge-yhm.2` — Beads change stream.
  - Needs Dolt commit polling validation.
  - Needs JSONL fallback and drift semantics.
  - Needs source-health model.
  - Needs batch handling.

**Concrete recommendation:** Ship GH first, then Beads. Keep shared WS envelope design so second bead plugs in cleanly.

---

### 8. Premortem — top 3 failures

#### 1. GitHub rate-limit exhaustion
Cause:
- Too many repos, too-fast polling, detail fetch storms.

Mitigation:
- ETags.
- Per-repo adaptive cadence.
- Global request budget.
- Backoff below 500 remaining.
- Poll active/tracked repos only.

#### 2. Stale Dolt source / wrong source chosen
Cause:
- Scanner marks Dolt “available” from config port, not actual query.
- Dolt server down while JSONL newer.
- Worktree JSONL edits invisible.

Mitigation:
- Health probe with actual SQL.
- Show source + freshness in UI.
- Compare Dolt commit vs `export-state.json`.
- Watch JSONL as drift signal, not canonical overwrite.
- Add “source degraded” event.

#### 3. WS storms / stale client state
Cause:
- Bulk close emits 50 events.
- reconnect resubscribe loops.
- Push arrives before DB transaction visible.
- Client mutates filtered arrays wrong.

Mitigation:
- Batch 1–2s.
- Watermarks.
- Client dedupe by stable ID.
- Refetch-on-gap.
- Exponential reconnect with jitter.
- Server heartbeat + max connection limits.

## Phase 2 — Devil's Advocate

Dolt commit polling can miss changes if bd writes without commit. Need working-set probe or `updated_at` fallback.

JSONL watcher can report backup export after Dolt already changed. If treated as source, stale overwrite risk. Must tag source and compare freshness.

bd hook seems attractive but brittle. Multi-process agents and direct file edits bypass it.

Per-repo GH polling may explode for large repo lists. Need budget gate before implementation, not after.

Separate WS channels create duplicate subscription logic. But existing client already subscribes by channel, so lower migration cost than unified channel.

Incremental client mutation risks filter bugs. Need refetch threshold and selected-detail refetch.

## Phase 3 — Synthesis

Best design is source-layered, not source-confused.

- GitHub: poll authoritative REST lists on cadence, store deltas, publish typed WS events.
- Beads: Dolt canonical when live; JSONL fallback and drift detector; batch changes.
- WS: separate operational channels, common event envelope.
- Client: optimistic incremental arrays with refetch-on-gap safety.

This keeps live feel without making WS canonical. DB/source remains truth. WS means “something changed; here is row or ID.”

## Phase 4 — Final Refined Output

## Final Answer

1. Dolt canonical. JSONL backup/export. Direct JSONL edits do not sync to Dolt without explicit import.
2. Beads detection rank: Dolt commit polling, JSONL watch fallback, bd hook later, Dolt CDC last.
3. GitHub: add per-repo issues + pulls polling, 5 min default, adaptive by repo count, ETag required.
4. WS: keep separate `github:activity` + `beads:changes`, shared typed envelope.
5. Cadence: 1–2s coalesced batches, flush cap, handle `xt end` bulk closes as one batch.
6. Client: upsert rows, refetch selected detail, full refetch only for large/gapped batches.
7. Split forge-yhm into GH first and Beads second.
8. Biggest risks: GH rate-limit, stale Dolt/source drift, WS/client state storms.

Final architecture in 5 lines:
- GH poller watches user events plus repo PR/issue updates with ETags.
- Beads watcher polls Dolt commit state and watches JSONL fallback.
- Server publishes typed batched events on source-specific WS channels.
- Client upserts small deltas and refetches on watermark gaps/big batches.
- Dolt remains canonical; JSONL never overwrites Dolt without explicit reconcile.

## Risks

1. **High:** Dolt/JSONL drift hides true issue state. Mitigate health probes + source freshness UI.
2. **High:** GitHub rate limit burns under many repos. Mitigate ETag + adaptive scheduler.
3. **Medium:** WS reconnect/render storms. Mitigate jitter + batches + dedupe.
4. **Medium:** Incremental store mutations break filtered views. Mitigate threshold refetch.
5. **Low:** bd hook misses clients. Use only optional latency booster.

## Verification

Read:
- `apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/api/ws/channels.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.ts`
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/github.ts`
- `apps/beadboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts`

- `apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts`
- `apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/stores/beads.ts`
- `.beads/metadata.json`
- `.beads/export-state.json`
- `docs/beadboard-inline-feed-spec.md`

## Beads

- Claim/notes/close not executed: no bash tool exposed.
- No files changed.
- No commit/push needed.

description:
PROBLEM
The original gitboard PR expanded view is leaner and more readable than the design's verbose version. Keep the leaner spine but cherry-pick the genuinely cool features from the design (octicon meta lines, dossier section labels, conversation thread with markers, file diff rows, linked-beads block). Drop chart-like features (lifecycle ribbon, progress bars, sparklines).

REFERENCES
- Current PR view: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/PrTimeline.tsx (526 lines)
- Design source: /tmp/xtrm-design/xtrm-console/project/src/git-module.jsx (look for PR dossier section)
- bd remember: xtrm-console-visual-contract

SUCCESS
- PR expanded view retains existing leaner layout (no two-column dossier sprawl).
- Adopts these design wins where they fit cleanly:
  * Octicon-marked conversation thread (already partially done by forge-hl1)
  * Section labels with octicon left-glyph (e.g. ChatIcon for Conversation, FileIcon for Files, BeadIcon for Linked beads)
  * File diff rows with +/- diff numbers in mono, kept simple (no visual meter bar)
  * Linked-beads block surfacing related forge-* IDs from PR body if present
- DROP: lifecycle ribbon (Opened → Review → Mergeable → Deployed), epic progress segmented bars, file diff visual meter bar, any other chart-like ornament.
- Preserve safe markdown rendering (existing pattern from recent commit "Render PR detail bodies safely").

SCOPE

- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/EventDetail.tsx (if it surfaces PR conversation)

NON_GOALS
- No icon-family changes (forge-hl1 done).
- No font-family changes (forge-m38 done).
- No token changes (forge-93p done).
- No charts, no progress bars, no sparklines, no lifecycle ribbons.
- Don't rewrite the layout from scratch — adapt incrementally.

CONSTRAINTS
- Preserve existing tests where they pertain to PrTimeline structure (or update them in lockstep).
- Markdown/HTML/XML rendering must remain XSS-safe (existing SafeMarkdown pattern).
- Section labels: 11-12px, uppercase tracking ~0.04em, Inter, accent-tinted octicon.

VALIDATION
- Snapshot the diff: changes localised to PrTimeline.tsx and at most 1-2 adjacent files.
- Visual: section labels use octicons; conversation thread items have type-marked octicons; no chart-like elements.

OUTPUT
- Manifest of design wins adopted vs deliberately skipped (with reason).
- Files changed, residual risks.

title: Octicon consistency pass across gitboard + beadboard
description:
PROBLEM
Mixed icon families across gitboard + beadboard: some files use @primer/octicons-react, others use unicode glyphs or custom SVGs. The xtrm-console contract requires one icon family — Octicons — everywhere.

REFERENCES
- Already in use: @primer/octicons-react (RepoSidebar, EventIcon, etc.)
- Design source icon set: /tmp/xtrm-design/xtrm-console/project/src/icons.jsx (octicon paths)
- bd remember: xtrm-console-visual-contract

SUCCESS
- Every visible glyph rendered via @primer/octicons-react components.
- No raw unicode glyphs in JSX text content used as icons (e.g. "✓", "●", "→", etc. used decoratively).
- No custom inline-SVG icons in components; if a needed glyph is missing from Octicons, document the exception.

SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/*.tsx
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/*.tsx
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx + apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx
- packages/ui/src/components/* if any icon-like components live there

NON_GOALS
- No font changes (forge-m38).
- No CSS token changes (forge-93p is landed).
- No layout changes.

CONSTRAINTS
- Match design's icon usage in meta lines: clock, person, branch, comment, file, diff, tag, type marks (bug/feature/task/epic/chore/decision/PR variants/issue variants).
- Sizing: design uses 12px for inline meta icons, 14px for row primary icons, 16px for headers.
- Preserve existing accessibility (aria-labels on icon buttons).

VALIDATION
- grep "•|●|→|✓|✗|⏵|⏷|◯" in JSX text content of scoped files — should return 0 matches for decorative use (any remaining must be text content, not icons).
- grep for raw <svg> in scoped files — should only appear via Octicon components.

OUTPUT
- Manifest of icon swaps per file.

title: Typography pass: less mono in UI text
description:
PROBLEM
JetBrains Mono is overused in UI text (titles, tabs, meta lines, labels). The xtrm-console contract restricts mono to IDs, SHAs, branch refs, diff numbers, and file paths — everything else uses Inter.

REFERENCES
- Design typography: /tmp/xtrm-design/xtrm-console/project/src/styles.css (font-family rules)
- bd remember: xtrm-console-visual-contract

SUCCESS
- Mono font-family used only on these surfaces: bead IDs (forge-xxx), PR numbers (#412), SHA hashes, branch refs (origin/main), diff numbers (+142/-28), file paths, code blocks, command hints.
- Everything else (titles, tabs, meta lines, dossier prose, kv keys, segment labels, state tokens, day headers) uses Inter.
- Hierarchy still readable — restore Inter where mono was masking intended emphasis.

SCOPE
- packages/ui/src/styles/tokens.css (typography tokens if needed)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css
- All *.tsx in apps/{gitboard,beadboard}/src/dashboard/components/* that set font-family inline

NON_GOALS
- No icon changes (forge-hl1).
- No layout reflow.
- No new tokens unless typography demands them.

CONSTRAINTS
- Don't add new font imports beyond Inter and JetBrains Mono.
- Preserve existing accessibility/contrast.
- Avoid mass replace — selectively change font-family in identified mono-leaking surfaces.

VALIDATION
- grep "JetBrains Mono\|monospace\|font-family.*mono" across scoped files — every remaining hit must be on one of the allowed surfaces (ID/SHA/branch/diff/path/code).

OUTPUT
- Manifest of font-family changes per file.
- List of surfaces that intentionally kept mono with the contract rule that justifies them.

A dedicated validation pass prevents another broad visual refactor from regressing functionality or routes.

## What to validate

Build both dashboards, restart preview, verify /gitboard and /beadboard independently, and inspect against docs/xtrm-console-visual-contract.md. File follow-up issues instead of expanding scope.

- [ ] bun run --cwd apps/gitboard build:dashboard passes.
- [ ] bun run --cwd apps/beadboard build:dashboard passes.
- [ ] /gitboard returns 200 and renders the Git view.
- [ ] /beadboard returns 200 and renders the Beads view.
- [ ] Visual contract checklist is reviewed.
- [ ] Follow-up issues are created for remaining drift.

## Approach notes

This task is validation only unless tiny style regressions are found. No architecture work.

## Context

Until a true shared shell exists, /gitboard and /beadboard remain separate routes. Their topbars and sidebars should still feel like the same product without embedding one app inside another.

## What to build

Align standalone topbars, tabs, sidebars, and module headers to the visual contract. Keep each route independent. Do not add /console, iframes, or a shared repo rail yet.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] /gitboard and /beadboard topbars use the same height, typography, border, background, and tab style.
- [ ] Sidebars use the same width, square rows, and border treatment.
- [ ] No duplicate shell chrome is introduced.
- [ ] Both dashboards build and routes return 200.

## Approach notes

  - Issue: sort rank omits valid status `in_review`, so those issues fall to unknown rank 99, after closed/deferred.

  - Suggested executor instruction: add `in_review` to rank/label map or derive rank from full status enum.

- Not judging broader product design, rail/kanban semantics, or backend payload shape. Outside sanity pass.

## Code Sanity Verdict
- Verdict: FINDINGS
- Scope reviewed: `HEAD 591c7a7` in `apps/beadboard/src/types/beads.ts`, `apps/beadboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts`, `apps/beadboard/src/core/beads-reader.ts`, `apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts`, `apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts`, `apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/lib/api.ts`
- Confidence: medium

## Findings
- Location: `apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts:108,124`
  - Issue: `new BeadsReader({} as any)` fake-constructs DB reader for pure JSONL routes.
  - Why it matters: `as any` hides contract break. Future constructor change or accidental DB access blows up at runtime, while type system gives false safety.
  - Suggested executor instruction: Split JSONL parsing helpers out of `BeadsReader`, or make `db` optional and stop passing fake object.

- Location: `apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts:80-83,191-217` plus `apps/beadboard/src/core/dolt-client.ts:57-67,113-132` and `apps/beadboard/src/core/beads-reader.ts:19-29`
  - Issue: issue-detail path returns source record with `project_id: ""`.
  - Why it matters: list route stamps `project_id`, detail route does not. API shape becomes inconsistent; downstream client can see empty project id even when route path already knows it.
  - Suggested executor instruction: Normalize detail payload before return: set `project_id` from route param in both Dolt/sqlite paths.

- Location: `apps/beadboard/src/core/beads-reader.ts:37-56,59-80`
  - Issue: JSONL parsers do `JSON.parse` per line with no per-line guard and then cast raw data straight to `Memory`/`Interaction`.
  - Why it matters: one malformed line drops whole file to empty array via outer catch, so bad record erases all good records. Also schema drift stays silent.
  - Suggested executor instruction: Parse line-by-line with try/catch, skip bad rows, and validate minimum fields before cast.

## Non-findings
- Did not flag fallback order; current `dolt -> sqlite -> jsonl -> unknown` chain looks sane from reviewed code.
- Did not validate build or clean worktree; `git status` in this session already showed unrelated dirty bead/serena files, and no build run here.

## Handoff
Fix 3 runtime/type holes above, then rerun reviewer. No broad refactor needed.

SCOPE: docs/beadboard-inline-feed-spec.md, apps/beadboard/src/core/*, apps/beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts, apps/beadboard/src/types/beads.ts, apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/*, relevant Gitboard FUI patterns in apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/github/* and apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css. REFERENCES: /home/dawid/dev/beads-viewer for bd CLI/detail patterns; /home/dawid/dev/beads_viewer for status/source/triage model. NON_GOALS: no destructive Beads mutations in first implementation slices; no reference repo edits; no broad Gitboard redesign beyond shared styling references. title: Console: stats from observability.db
description:
PROBLEM
xt specialists run produce a local observability.db (SQLite) with timings, token spend, payload sizes, status, model. No UI surface; users have to run sp ps or query SQL directly.

REFERENCES
- Local DB likely lives at .specialists/observability.db or .xtrm/observability.db (VERIFY before implementing — first step of the SCOPE)
- KPI skill: bd memories using-kpi (background: KPI analysis on observability)
- Existing stat-tile primitive: TriageStatCard / TriageStatGrid in apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx
- bun:sqlite for read-only access
- apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts mounts /api/console (per forge-z6j)

SUCCESS
- New read-only API: /api/console/observability/jobs (recent N jobs), /api/console/observability/roles (per-role: count, avg duration, avg tokens, p50/p95), /api/console/observability/spend (per-day token + cost rollup).
- Observability module in /console renders: spend last 7/30 days · per-role table · recent jobs list (id, role, bead, status, duration, tokens) · waiting jobs (status='waiting').
- All read-only; no kill/resume actions in v1.

SCOPE
- VERIFY (step 1): exact path of observability.db on disk; confirm tables/columns. Likely tables: jobs(id, role, bead, status, started_at, ended_at, model), turns(job_id, tokens_in, tokens_out, cost_usd). VERIFY-then-implement.
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/observability.ts (new)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/observability-store.ts (new — bun:sqlite reader with prepared statements)
- apps/console/src/.../Observability.tsx (new — or beadboard tab if /console not yet)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/client.ts (getObservabilityJobs / getRoles / getSpend)
- apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/observability.test.ts (new)

NON_GOALS
- No write actions (no killing jobs, no editing rows).
- No re-running of past jobs.
- No reading other plugins' DBs.

CONSTRAINTS
- Read-only mode (open DB with readonly: true).
- DB path resolved via env or config — do not hardcode.
- If DB does not exist: API returns empty arrays / zeros, not 500.
- Numbers shown in human-readable form (1.2k tokens, $0.034) but raw values available in response JSON.

VALIDATION
- bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard test green; new endpoint test handles missing DB gracefully.
- /api/console/observability/jobs returns ≥0 items.
- Manual: KPIs render on Observability tab; numbers match a direct sqlite3 query.

OUTPUT
- Confirmed DB path + schema (the actual verification finding), API contract, files changed, screenshot of Observability tab.

description:

REFERENCES

- Filter chips: All · Running · Waiting · Done.
- Refreshes every 5s while module is visible (clearable interval; not polling when hidden).

SCOPE

- apps/gitboard/src/core/specialists-runtime.ts (new — invoke sp ps --json with execFile, 2s timeout)

NON_GOALS

CONSTRAINTS

VALIDATION

- Manual: kick off a specialist via xt-end-style flow; UI shows the running job within 5s; click → bead-notes turn log renders.

OUTPUT

title: Beadboard: replace remaining bv parity gaps with native scoring
description:
PROBLEM
The Triage tab currently scores issues with a small client-side heuristic: priority weight (P0=1 ... P4=0) + staleness (daysIdle/30 capped at 1) + open-load (openCount/100 capped at 1). bv --robot-triage uses a richer ranker: pagerank centrality, betweenness, blocker_ratio, time_to_impact, urgency aging curve, risk signals (fan_variance, activity_churn, cross_repo_risk, status_risk, composite). The dashboard does NOT shell out to bv at runtime, so the heuristic gap is intentional today, but rank quality on large boards (≥500 issues) is noticeably weaker than bv.

REFERENCES
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx — getTriageSignals, buildTriageView
- bv --robot-triage JSON shape (already inspected): score breakdown includes pagerank/betweenness/blocker_ratio/staleness/priority_boost/time_to_impact/urgency/risk
- bd dependency edges available client-side from issue.dependencies

SUCCESS
A DECISION bead. Two acceptable outcomes:

(a) PORT: implement pagerank + betweenness over the loaded dependency graph (Map<id, BeadIssue> → adjacency), feed into the score blend at weights {pagerank: 0.25, betweenness: 0.10, blocker_ratio: 0.10, priority: 0.20, staleness: 0.15, urgency: 0.15, risk: 0.05}. Add risk_signals.status_risk computed from status (blocked=0.6, in_progress=0.3, open=0.1, closed=0). No external deps; pure JS graph algorithms (~150 lines).
OR
(b) ACCEPT: keep current heuristic, document the parity gap as intentional in the bead description, and add a 'View in bv' hint in the Triage panel (link to copy-pasteable 'bv --robot-triage' command).

Either way, the dashboard never executes 'bv' as a subprocess; bv stays a design reference + CLI for power users.

SCOPE (if (a) is chosen)
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/App.tsx — replace getTriageSignals body
- apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/lib/graph.ts (new — pagerank + betweenness)
- apps/beadboard/tests/dashboard/lib/graph.test.ts (new — unit tests for both algorithms on small graphs)

NON_GOALS
- Do not import bv as a library.
- Do not shell to bv.
- Do not change Velocity / Project Health tiles.

CONSTRAINTS
- Algorithms must be O(V·E) or better; iteration cap 25 for pagerank with damping 0.85; reject any algorithm needing external libraries.
- Score normalization must keep the existing top-5 cap.

VALIDATION (if (a))
- Unit tests for pagerank on hand-drawn graphs (triangle, star, chain).
- Unit tests for betweenness on a 5-node graph with a known bridge.
- Triage top-3 on the local board includes the previously-bv-flagged issues (regression sample = top 3 from bv --robot-triage at time of land).

OUTPUT
- Decision: (a) PORT or (b) ACCEPT, with rationale.
- If (a): files changed, tests added, top-3 comparison.
- If (b): bead description updated with parity gap rationale.
acceptance_criteria:
Decision recorded as either:
(a) PORT — pagerank + betweenness implemented in apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/lib/graph.ts; getTriageSignals uses new weight blend; unit tests pass for pagerank (triangle/star/chain) and betweenness (5-node with known bridge); Triage top-3 overlaps bv --robot-triage top-5 on the regression sample
(b) ACCEPT — bead description updated with parity-gap rationale; Triage panel surfaces 'Run bv --robot-triage' copy-pasteable hint; no code changes
Either way: dashboard never executes 'bv' as a subprocess

value: Beadboard kanban column-width inconsistency root cause: StatusColumn used minWidth:240/maxWidth:300 as flex children with default flex:0 1 auto, so flexbox sized each column based on internal content — short-titled columns settled at minWidth, longer-content columns grew toward maxWidth. Fix: flex:'1 1 0' + minWidth:260 (no maxWidth). Now all columns share parent space equally and minWidth only kicks in when total < parent width (triggers horizontal scroll instead of squashing).

All three of expanded/selection/sidebarCollapsed persist to localStorage under forge-5w9:expanded/selection/sidebarCollapsed keys. Narrow selectors (selectActiveSection, selectRepos, selectExpanded, selectSidebarCollapsed) avoid re-renders. Aggregator hook useRepoTree() in apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useRepoTree.ts: matches beads projects to github repos via TAIL of full_name (e.g. Jaggerxtrm/foo ↔ beads project 'foo'). 18 matched out of 69 github + 28 beads on this machine.

Gitboard expanded-body card pattern: wrap .pr-expanded-body and .issue-expanded-body with --card-border, --card-radius-md (8px), --surface-secondary bg, 14-18px padding, small 4-6px margin. This creates GitHub-style 'card floating on darker page' effect. Page bg --surface-primary (#0d1117) is darker than --surface-secondary (#161b22), so the card pops. Watch for later .pr-expanded-body override rules in globals.css (~line 1974) — must keep them aligned with first rule (~line 1819).

xtrm-console typography pass: keep JetBrains Mono only on IDs, SHAs, branch refs, diff numbers, file paths, code blocks, and command hints; Inter everywhere else in dashboard chrome/meta text.

Beadboard PR auto-link: client-side substring match of beads issue.id (regex with word-boundary lookahead/behind, escape dots for epic children like 'forge-z6j.1') against OPEN PR title+body from /api/github/prs. Stored github_prs has no head_ref column, so branch-based matching would require schema+poller change — title/body matching is enough for repos using 'Closes <id>' convention.

forge-5w9 reviewer waiver (forge-lec): getOpenPrs intentionally NOT ported into beadsApi. Rationale: it's a /api/github/prs endpoint, not /api/beads. gitboard's own apiClient.getPrs covers github PR fetches. prByIssueId is OPTIONAL in BeadCard/StatusColumn/KanbanBoard component props — never wired = no runtime regression. Future PR-link feature should reshape apiClient.getPrs() result into OpenPr at the wiring layer, not duplicate the fetcher in beads-api. Reviewer score 89/PARTIAL — accepted with this waiver.
xtrm console now uses /console as the primary shell, backed by /api/console/repos for merged GitHub/Beads repo records; /gitboard and /beadboard remain legacy mounted routes during migration.

Beadboard /api/beads/projects/<id>/issues returns BeadDependency entries with title='' (the reader/exporter doesn't denormalise dep titles). Clients must resolve dep titles by id from the loaded issues list rather than trusting dep.title. Same for dep.status — prefer issueById.get(dep.id)?.status. Source: forge-3k5 dependency tree fix.
Beadboard kanban dossier markdown should strip raw HTML tags and whitelist link protocols before rendering inline.

forge-ci9 sidebar third-level nav: SidebarSelection is { repo, section, leaf }. Expanded set tracks BOTH repo (key=fullName) AND section (key='fullName::github' or '::beads'). Leaves drive MainPane to a single full-content view per click. Per-leaf component mapping in MainPane.tsx: activity→ActivityTimeline, prs→PrTimeline, issues→IssueTimeline, releases→ReleaseTimeline, kanban/issues→BeadsRepoView. Black bg via #000 !important on body/.shell-app/.shell-main/.shell-sidebar. Collapse-toggle works because title is conditionally rendered (omitted when collapsed) so flex layout doesn't push the 20px toggle out of the 48px width.

xtrm-console visual contract: bg #181818, single accent #8ed2dc, radius 0 (no pills), thin rgba(255,255,255,0.07–0.18) borders, no gradients, Inter+JetBrains Mono with mono restricted to IDs/SHAs/branch refs/diff numbers/file paths, @primer/octicons-react as the only icon family, no charts/heatmaps/sparklines. Source: design handoff gDsOk8IG1FcsM7ojcdFJNg.

forge-5w9 security finding patched (forge-bvq): added encodeURIComponent on every path-segment interpolation of projectId/issueId in apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/lib/beads-api.ts. Risk was path-confusion (no XSS sink). LOW severity, fix applied same session as audit.

Gitboard Beads Feed UI rules: active issues render blocker tree with single primary blocker placement to avoid duplicate rows; epic relation propagates purple markers through nested descendants; right column format is 'P<n> TYPE status date / deps / PR / agent' with only priority/type colored by type (bug red, epic purple, feature royal blue).

Realtime envelope version is STRING not number — initial forge-dgd shipped with version:number + Date.parse coercion, which silently fell back to REALTIME_PROTOCOL_VERSION for non-numeric strings (e.g. Dolt commit hashes), breaking dedupe for beads. Caught during forge-f0g review. Now: version is the source's row updated_at ISO (github) or commit hash (beads), passed through verbatim. Default for unversioned publishes is String(REALTIME_PROTOCOL_VERSION).
Review run: 14d1e1 compliance audit completed with traceability evidence and build verification

When sp reviewer or sp merge complains about huge scope ('540 files changed') vs a much smaller claimed scope, suspect a stale local main: the merge-base used by the reviewer is whatever's in your local refs, and if local main is far behind origin/main (or behind a recently-merged PR), the cumulative diff balloons. Workaround: ignore the scope-breach finding when you can confirm the executor's own diff (git log <branch> ^HEAD --oneline) is small, and patch-apply the executor's actual commits directly. Source: forge-5wc and forge-pq6 reviewers both flagged 540-file scope while real executor diffs were 2 and 3 files.
bv triage score uses pagerank, staleness, priority_boost, time_to_impact, urgency, and risk; top picks can be mirrored client-side with open issue idle time, priority cost, and open load.

forge-7xu unified shell: TopBar [GitHub|Beads] switch + tabs (7 github / 5 beads) + Sidebar single-level repos with RepoIcon + MainPane per-(surface,tab) dispatch. All 5 beads panels live in BeadsRepoView.tsx (Kanban=KanbanBoard, Feed=IssueFeed, Triage=inline TriagePanel ported from beadboard App.tsx:411, Closed=inline list, Memories=inline list). GitHub README/CHANGELOG/Reports use ReadmeView/ChangelogView/ReportsView from RepoContentPanels.tsx. CSS class prefix .ide-* in globals.css. Black bg via .ide-shell.

sp merge fails with bookkeeping conflicts when the specialist's executor branch is based on a snapshot where .beads/ files don't exist as currently tracked. Workaround: git cherry-pick -X theirs <commits>, then 'git checkout HEAD -- .beads/' to restore bookkeeping, then 'git rm --cached' any '.beads~<hash>' conflict-marker entries before committing. The executor's design changes land clean; only .beads/ infra needs preservation. Source: forge-93p merge session.

Beadboard descriptions sometimes contain literal JSON-escape sequences (the two characters backslash+n) instead of real newlines, because some bd callers (planner specialists, copy-pasted prompts, mol templates) store JSON-encoded content as the description verbatim. Any markdown renderer must run a replace(/\\n/g, '\\n')-style unescape before splitting on newlines or the body renders as a single line with visible backslash-n. Source: forge-zg3 description rendering fix.

Markdown table parsing: pipe-row + separator row (|---|---|) is the trigger. Reject early if next line isn't dashes-with-pipes pattern. parseTableCells strips leading/trailing | then splits on | and trims. Fenced code blocks: detect (```|~~~) + optional lang, consume lines until matching fence.

bd dep rule: an epic cannot block a task (bd dep add <epic-id> <task-id> errors with 'epics can only block other epics, not tasks'). Use --parent on bd create instead to nest tasks under an epic, OR use bd dep relate for non-blocking links between epic and task. Tasks can block tasks. Epics can block epics. Source: forge-qct epic linking session.

When dispatching a 'structural alignment' executor that rewrites a row/dossier layout, verify field completeness in the new output before merging. forge-pq6 silently dropped IssueDossier's Dependencies, Children, Labels, Agent and replaced the markdown renderer with stripHtml(line)→

; the regression wasn't visible until a user expanded a bead. Reviewer-PASS on the structural shape doesn't catch dropped data because the contract names the shape, not the dossier sections. Mitigation: in structural-alignment bead contracts, list every section/affordance that must survive the refactor under a 'PRESERVE' bullet.

Beads watcher (forge-igg) diffs 4 entity snapshots per Dolt commit-hash tick (issues, deps, memories, kv) and emits per-entity events plus semantic aliases (issue.deferred/superseded/flagged/unflagged) derived from field transitions. JSONL fallback only covers issues.jsonl — memories/kv/deps degrade to stale while Dolt down, surfaced via beads:source_health {drift:true, scope:...}. Watcher version = Dolt commit hash (string); no Bun.sleep — use setTimeout for Node-test compat.
Beadboard feed now uses shared module-header/row spine structure; filter toggle wired to local open-only state.

gitboard UI architecture: packages/ui/components is empty placeholder; all gitboard styling is app-local via inline styles + globals.css. Radius tokens all 0px by design. Three structural gaps in current shell: (1) type scale 11-18px is dense, (2) body{overflow:hidden}+fixed sidebar/detail widths cause horizontal zoom overflow, (3) no enclosure system on cards. Three executor beads touch tokens.css so must sequence A→B→C.
GitHub Contents API: GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents/{path} returns base64 file content for files OR array of entries for dirs. Decode with Buffer.from(content, 'base64'). 404 means missing file.

Functional shell to refine: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/{components/shell,components/beads/BeadsRepoView,components/github/GithubRepoView,styles/globals.css}.

Unified expanded-row card pattern: when .pr-row.is-expanded (or .issue-row.is-expanded), strip bottom radius+border on row and zero its margin-bottom; on the .pr-expanded-body (or .issue-expanded-body), strip top border+radius. Result: row+body render as a single card with full rounded outer corners only. Required because the row and body are separate DOM siblings — without this they look like two stacked cards.

Custom .release-body-text style produced unstyled markdown output (no h3/list/p spacing).

Build config gotchas in this monorepo: (1) packages/ui/src/styles/tokens.css must NOT import 'tailwindcss' — packages/ui has no node_modules; the consumer app's globals.css does the import. (2) apps/beadboard uses vitest with globals:false, so tests/dashboard/setup.ts must explicitly 'import { expect } from vitest' and call expect.extend(matchers) — side-effect import of @testing-library/jest-dom fails. Setup file must be registered in vitest.config.ts setupFiles. (3) apps/beadboard/tests/core/beads-reader.test.ts imports bun:sqlite which vitest can't resolve — that test needs to migrate to 'bun test' runner or be split out of the vitest include. Source: post-design-refresh build fixup.

Beads UI affordance gaps vs bd CLI: 'bd sync' command does NOT exist (design buttons referencing it must use bd import or be dropped); bd does support comments, search, defer/undefer, reopen, create, history, graph — these are absent from the xtrm-console design but should be added when the UI lands. Source: forge-tm0 explorer audit job e75d94.

Realtime envelope uses boot_id (uuid per server start), NOT process-start timestamp, so resume can detect server restarts. Client sends {since_seq, boot_id}; server replays from ring buffer only if boot_id matches AND seq in range, else emits domain sync_hint. Process timestamp would conflate clock skew with restart; uuid is explicit. Buffer is in-memory (500 envelopes/channel), wiped on restart — full state is rebuilt via sync_hint, not persisted.

When briefing executor for additions to existing apps, ENFORCE 'reuse existing primitive X at file:path; do NOT scaffold new chrome' in CONSTRAINTS — and list the specific component to wrap or extend. Past gap: forge-z6j.1 said 'reuse RepoSidebar/IssueFeed/PrTimeline' in soft NON_GOALS/CONSTRAINTS prose; executor still built parallel primitives. Phrase as imperative: 'IMPORT and MOUNT <Component> from <file>; do not write a replacement.'
Beadboard dossier audit log now fetches interactions lazily on expansion; stale quick filter uses 7-day updated_at threshold.

CSS grid + position:absolute child gotcha: an absolutely-positioned child of a 'display: grid' container is REMOVED from grid flow, so its template column gets filled by the NEXT in-flow child. If your design has N grid-template-columns but you include a position:absolute decoration as one of the spans, you actually have N-1 in-flow children filling N columns — everything shifts left and columns visually misalign (e.g. id renders in the rail slot, title renders in the id slot). Two fixes: either drop the absolute span (use a ::before pseudo instead) or keep it in-flow with grid-column:1. Source: forge-pq6 beadboard rows where <span className='rail' /> with position:absolute caused id+title to overlap until forge-xsk.
Beadboard project summaries now carry source health and priority; issue detail endpoint falls back from Dolt to sqlite when live Dolt unavailable.

Design handoffs from Claude Design (Anthropic) use --surface-1/2/3 and --danger token names, but this repo's packages/ui/src/styles/tokens.css uses --surface-primary/secondary/tertiary and --accent-red. When porting design selectors that reference --surface-1/2/3 or --danger, either rename the selector to use the repo's tokens OR add aliases at the top of tokens.css. Failure to do this silently breaks any element using those vars (background collapses to transparent). Source: forge-pq6 structural alignment broke beadboard until forge-qct alias commit.

forge-5w9.4 sidebar component: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/Sidebar.tsx. Flat visible-row list approach for ARIA tree (no recursion). focusedId state synced via onFocus handler — critical to avoid stale-state when DOM focus moves out-of-band (e.g. tests calling .focus()). CSS in globals.css under '/* forge-5w9: IDE shell sidebar */' marker. Preview route /gitboard/shell-preview in App.tsx (ShellPreview component) — REMOVE in forge-5w9.6 once MainPane lands.
PR detail view now uses section-label + file-row patterns; linked beads extracted from forge-* refs

renderPrBodyText only handled links + inline code originally; added **bold** + *italic* + .pr-rich-text h3/p/ul/li/strong/em/code styling to make GH release/PR notes render with proper visual hierarchy. Regex uses negative lookbehind `(?<![*\\w])` to prevent italic from eating bold markers.

bd swarm integrates with specialists-v3 as the orchestration loop: validate epic → create swarm molecule → poll 'bd swarm status --json' for Ready fronts → dispatch 'sp run executor --worktree --bead <id> --background' per ready issue, reviewer via --job <exec-job>. Replaces hand-rolled bd ready polling with epic-scoped, wave-grouped status. v3 bead contracts/job rules unchanged.

CSS grid implicit-column trap: display:grid WITHOUT grid-template-columns creates an implicit single-column grid sized to fit-content, not fill the parent. Children shrink to the width of the LARGEST row content, not to the container. Symptom: percentage widths on grandchildren resolve inconsistently because the parent row is variable width. Fix: always specify grid-template-columns (e.g. minmax(0, 1fr)) on a single-column list grid, OR use display:flex; flex-direction:column. Source: forge-l85 beadboard .module-list inconsistent dossier width.

Gitboard PR/issue expanded view style follows GitHub per-entry pattern, NOT a single outer wrapping card. Each .pr-conversation-entry (commit, comment, review, body) renders as its own discrete card with --card-border, --card-radius-sm or -md, --surface-secondary bg. .pr-expanded-body itself has NO border/bg — it's just a flat container. Body/comment/review entries get larger padding + --card-radius-md; commit/event entries stay smaller with --card-radius-sm. This iteration corrected an earlier mistake where the whole expanded view was wrapped in one outer card frame.

forge-5w9 routing decision: Zustand store-driven sidebar selection swap, no router lib. Rationale: no router lib in package.json; only 2 routes per repo; finite repo list; hash router is ~10 LOC future-add if URL sharing becomes needed. SidebarSelection type lives in apps/gitboard/src/types/shell.ts.

gitboard already mounts beadboard's /api/beads/* routes via apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts:6,35 (imports beadsRoutes from '../../../beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts'). Cross-package import. forge-5w9.9 deprecation MUST move beadboard's api/routes/beads.ts + core/beads-*.ts into apps/gitboard before deleting beadboard, or gitboard's beads endpoints break. beadboard backend on port 3010 is NOT a runtime dependency for gitboard — gitboard serves beads itself on its own port (3000).
Density pass: shrink spacing by token-level padding/gap changes, not font size; keep row height via --row-height and topbar via --topbar-height.

forge-0w4 backend gotchas: (1) /api/beads/projects/{id}/issues/closed returns 404 on Dolt-backed projects — fetch ALL via /issues?limit=1000 and split client-side. (2) /api/github/events default limit is 50 (most-recent across ALL repos), /api/github/prs is 100 — client-side filter-by-repo on the global store returns 0 for most repos. Solution: per-repo apiClient.getEvents({repos:[fullName]}) + getPrs({repo}) + getIssues({repo}) + getReleases({repo}) in a local hook (useGithubRepoData in MainPane.tsx).
review-forge-hl1-partial-missing-chevrondownicon-import

Specialist reviewers in gitboard validate their own worktree, not uncommitted main-worktree diffs; for self-implemented changes, commit or provide a branch-visible artifact before dispatching reviewer, otherwise reviews fail on missing lineage/diff evidence.

forge-qc0 layout invariant: ANY MainPane content that uses @tanstack/react-virtual (ActivityTimeline, PrTimeline, IssueTimeline, ReleaseTimeline) needs a SIZED parent. Required CSS chain: html/body/#root height:100%; .ide-shell height:100vh + overflow:hidden; .ide-body flex:1 + min-height:0; .ide-main flex:1 + min-height:0 + overflow:hidden; .ide-main > * flex:1 + min-height:0. Without this chain virtualizer reports container as zero-height and renders nothing. min-height:100vh on shell is NOT enough — must be height:100vh.
